title: Prevent duplicate dev drafts from the content workbench CTA
description:
## Problem
Primary workbench może wywołać kolejne runExecutionLive mimo że activation packet potwierdza istniejący dev draft. Existing-draft update jest jawnie unsupported, więc CTA może tylko duplikować szkice albo wprowadzać operatora w błąd.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Otwórz homepage work item z draft_readback.status=available. 2. Primary canCreateEditedDevDraft ignoruje draftReadback, a sticky CTA nadal wybiera runExecutionLive, gdy istnieje writeAuthorization. 3. Dolny legacy panel ma guard hasCreatedDevDraft, którego top nie zachował.

## Current Evidence
ContentWorkflowSurface.tsx odczytuje draftReadback, ale primary CTA i canCreateEditedDevDraft nie blokują create. Live dev post 1275 istnieje, update_supported=false.

## Out of Scope
Implementacja update istniejącego draftu/ACF, publish i zmiana public canonical.

## Dependencies and Tests
Po c9h9.3. Testy: Vitest mock negative call, existing/no-existing draft cases i browser E2E.

## Done
Gdy draft istnieje, first-screen CTA otwiera istniejący szkic lub pokazuje preview-only blocker; POST live create nie jest wywoływany.
design: Wydzielić DevDraftPrimaryAction oparty wyłącznie o activation packet i existing-update readiness. Reużyć typed links/status; nie inferować możliwości update. Create pozostaje dostępne tylko bez istniejącego draftu i później przez canonical apply path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Existing draft zmienia CTA na Otwórz istniejący szkic. 2. Execution live mutation nie jest wywołana w existing-draft state. 3. Edycje pozostają preview-only i pokazują API blocker update. 4. Create case bez draftu zachowuje typed readiness. 5. Focused Vitest, E2E i screenshot przechodzą.
notes: 2026-07-10 resolved by the stronger fail-closed slice: ContentWorkflowSurface has no runExecutionLive call or create-new-dev-draft CTA. Existing draft remains available through Otwórz podgląd na dev/Otwórz stronę dev; edits and primary CTA are preview-only and gated by API dry_run_ready/execution_blockers. Adversarial Vitest proves no direct live request; current desktop screenshot has no duplicate-create CTA. Future create-without-existing-draft behavior now belongs solely to canonical apply c9h9.4.
close_reason: Superseded by the stronger fail-closed boundary: no direct create CTA or live mutation remains; existing draft is opened/previewed and future create is owned by c9h9.4 canonical apply.

title: Remove cold-load waterfall from content workflow
description:
## Problem
Cold open /content-workflow pokazuje globalne Ładowanie stanu WILQ przez około 23 s. Operator traci prawie całe 30-sekundowe okno przed zobaczeniem decyzji.

## Current Evidence
Browser resource timings: queue 10.4 s; po queue snapshot 11.7 s, enrichment 11.8 s i activation 12.6 s; po snapshot existing-draft readiness 6.4 s. Kod czeka na queue, potem pełny snapshot, a router wielokrotnie uruchamia build_content_diagnostics.

## Out of Scope
Ogólny refaktor wszystkich diagnostics, nowy endpoint bez dowodu potrzeby i mechaniczny split całego route.

## Dependencies and Tests
Po c9h9.5 freshness, aby progressive first card miała prawdziwy stan. Powiązane r564/ho41. Testy: deferred-promise Vitest, request/build count, Playwright cold timing, desktop/mobile loading state.

## Done
Pierwsza API-owned decision card pojawia się lokalnie w maksymalnie 5 s, dolne dane ładują się lokalnie bez blank global loadera, a initial selected item nie buduje pełnego diagnostics wielokrotnie.
design: Reużyć istniejące queue/snapshot/enrichment service contracts. Najpierw renderować queue-owned decision shell i lokalne stany ładowania. Pogłębić wspólny diagnostics build/cache seam na czas jednego initial request flow zamiast dodawać kolejny endpoint. Mierzyć cold browser network trace.
acceptance_criteria: 1. First decision visible <=5 s na managed stack albo konkretny zewnętrzny blocker jest zapisany. 2. Brak globalnego blank loadera podczas snapshot/enrichment/authoring reads. 3. Najwyżej jeden pełny diagnostics build dla początkowego wybranego work itemu. 4. Subordinate panels mają lokalne loading/error states. 5. Focused API/dashboard tests, E2E timing i desktop/mobile screenshots przechodzą. 6. Brak nowej logiki biznesowej w React.
notes: 2026-07-10 stronger live proof: focused Playwright content-workflow-layout times out at 30s waiting for selected snapshot response, and the second dashboard content E2E fails on the same cold waterfall. Managed API remains healthy; warm browser screenshot eventually renders. This confirms the product blocker and must not be hidden by raising E2E timeouts.
close_reason: Closed 2026-07-11: first content decision is visible within the queue budget before subordinate workflow reads; diagnostics builds are reused/prewarmed, loading/error states are local, and browser/API/test proof passes.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 freshness handoff: stale primary proof now blocks the selected queue candidate and avoids starting the expensive snapshot query; focused content E2E validates the blocker in 13.5s. Prior cold selected-snapshot waterfall proof remains the performance acceptance target.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Closed 2026-07-11: queue/snapshot cold waterfall resolved with short-lived content diagnostics cache (cleared with view-model mutations), reuse of one content metric-fact read by tactical queue, API startup prewarm (fail-open), and queue-owned first decision card with local snapshot loading/error states. Live proof after managed restart: queue 0.023s after prewarm; live queue remains blocked/stale at 0/2 actionable. Focused E2E enforces queue <5s and no global Ładowanie stanu WILQ; Playwright 1 passed, dashboard Vitest 138/138, typecheck/build/lint, focused backend content tests, Ruff/mypy green. Complexity: 10 changed files, 2 documented frozen growth files, 2 local budget violations in content diagnostics. Browser proof: .local-lab/proof/independent-review-2026-07-11/content-workflow-c9h9-6-desktop-1536x1024.png and mobile-390x844.png.

title: Carry current freshness into content decisions and evidence
description:
## Problem
Kanoniczna content queue usuwa typed freshness z ContentDiagnosticsResponse, liczy stale sygnały jako zwykły actionable refresh i pozwala first screen pokazać zielone GSC w dowodach. Historyczne evidence z completed refresh również pozostaje oznaczone fresh mimo że connector jest stale.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Odczytaj /api/connectors i potwierdź stale GSC, WordPress i Ahrefs. 2. Odczytaj /api/content/diagnostics: freshness_assessment.state=stale i requires_refresh=true. 3. Odczytaj /api/content/work-items/queue: brak freshness/missing contracts, kandydat homepage ma refresh/plan_allowed i jest actionable. 4. Otwórz /content-workflow: GSC ma zielone w dowodach, a globalny blocker/safe refresh step nie jest above fold.

## Current Evidence
ContentWorkItemQueueResponse w wilq/content/workflow/queue.py mapuje decision_queue, ale nie diagnostics.freshness_assessment. ContentSourceStatusBar inferuje obecność connectora. wilq/evidence/registry.py mapuje historyczny completed refresh na fresh bez bieżącego age check.

## Out of Scope
Automatyczny refresh job, nowy endpoint, publikacja i przebudowa całego content UI.

## Dependencies and Tests
Powiązane z wilq-seo-4wwo, r564 i c9h9.2, ale żaden nie naprawia semantyki freshness. Testy: fresh/stale/missing/blocked; current evidence resolution; queue↔snapshot decision consistency; shared Zod; first-screen stale blocker i safe next step.

## Done
Każda content recommendation pokazuje current freshness/missing contracts i bezpieczny next step; stale primary proof nie jest traktowany jako bieżąca actionable authoring decision.
design: Rozszerzyć istniejący ContentWorkItemQueueResponse/Candidate lub współdzielony content decision view-model o reuse ContentFreshnessAssessment i typed metric/query facts. API, nie React, ustala status, blocker i safe next step. Evidence registry musi odróżniać freshness w chwili kolekcji od current/as-of freshness. Nie dodawać endpointu ani lokalnego translatora.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Queue i selected snapshot expose spójny recommended_mode/reason/freshness/blocker/safe_next_step. 2. stale/missing/blocked primary sources obniżają actionability i wymagają refresh-first. 3. Evidence ID resolution nie nazywa historycznego completed run wiecznie fresh. 4. First viewport pokazuje current source state i global queue blocker bez raw payload. 5. Regex/prose extraction nie jest źródłem decyzji. 6. Backend/shared schema/dashboard tests, live stale API proof i diff check przechodzą.
close_reason: Closed 2026-07-11: queue and selected snapshots now carry typed ContentFreshnessAssessment plus the queue candidate; stale primary GSC/public WordPress proof adds content_sources_require_refresh, changes actionability to block, preserves evidence/source lineage, and exposes refresh-first next_step above fold. Verified live HTTP queue/snapshot, 4 focused backend content test files, 31 shared schema tests, dashboard typecheck/focused Vitest, and desktop/mobile agent-browser renders.

title: Restore dev-only WordPress draft creation through canonical ActionObject apply
description:
## Problem
Po fail-closed naprawie WILQ potrzebuje jednej kanonicznej ścieżki tworzenia szkicu na dev: validate → preview → human review → confirm → audit → vendor adapter. Obecny centralny act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff deklaruje adapter boundary, ale mutation-readiness nadal ma apply_allowed=false/api_mutation_ready=false, a jego adapter wykonuje tylko dry-run bez zatwierdzonego handoff/draft package.

## Current Evidence
/api/actions/mutation-readiness raportuje vendor_write_possible_count=0. Live write wcześniej działał poza tym mechanizmem. Adapter WordPress nie egzekwuje jawnie roli hosta dev i może użyć dowolnego skonfigurowanego base URL.

## Out of Scope
Publikacja, aktualizacja istniejącego wpisu/ACF, delete i production WordPress writes.

## Dependencies and Tests
Zależne od wilq-seo-c9h9.3. Testy muszą objąć exact action/operation/work item/handoff/draft package/target/actor binding, rejected/deferred review, dev/public/arbitrary host, mutation audit i zero HTTP dla blockerów.

## Done
Jedyną klasą vendor write jest create_wordpress_draft na zatwierdzonym dev hoście przez apply-capable ActionObject; każdy adapter attempt ma spójny ActionMutationAudit i activation packet.
design: Seam: pogłębić istniejący moduł ActionObject mutation adapter dla act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff. Adapter dostaje typed zatwierdzony target content packet, sprawdza rolę dev hosta przed HTTP i zapisuje redacted result/audit. Content router nie odzyskuje własnej niezależnej autoryzacji. Nie dodawać endpointu ani drugiej ścieżki write.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Tylko act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff z mode=apply, allowed_operation=create_wordpress_draft i kompletnym ActionObject audit trail może zbudować typed WordPressDraftApplyCapability i dojść do adaptera; capability wiąże exact action/work item/handoff/draft package/target/actor, zamiast ufać surowym booleansom payloadu. 2. Publiczny ekologus.pl, dowolny niezatwierdzony host i existing post update są blokowane przed HTTP. 3. Vendor attempt/result tworzy ActionMutationAudit; activation packet pokazuje prawdziwe external_write_attempted. 4. Publish/destructive pozostają false. 5. Focused action/content/API/dashboard tests, Ruff, mypy, browser proof i diff check przechodzą. 6. Live write pozostaje poza UI do czasu potwierdzenia bieżącego review/audytu i gotowości operatora.
notes:
2026-07-10 handoff from safety review: direct UI and content router live paths are removed. Canonical apply must replace raw action_apply_authorized/write_authorization_verified booleans with a typed capability created inside apply_action and bind exact apply action, work item, handoff, draft package, target host and audit actor. It must also restore any create CTA only through central apply; current UI intentionally exposes preview-only. Existing tests named after_action_apply currently model the lower boundary with manual booleans and are not proof of central apply.
2026-07-11 rebaseline: original acceptance requiring payload apply_allowed=true/api_mutation_ready=true was stale relative to the typed-capability seam. The canonical apply now derives authorization from current snapshot/review/audit and exact typed request; static payload booleans remain fail-closed and are not sufficient. Route integration proof 06cf7dfb passes with adapter reached only after capability binding.
close_reason: Canonical dev-only WordPress draft creation is implemented through typed ActionWordPressDraftApplyInput and WordPressDraftApplyCapability inside apply_action. Route integration proves exact work item/handoff/draft package/canonical URL/confirm actor binding, preview/confirm/impact audit, ActionMutationAudit and adapter reach; public/arbitrary hosts block before HTTP; publish/destructive remain false; shared dashboard contract, review-only CTA, focused tests, browser proof, Ruff/mypy and typecheck are green. Live write remains intentionally unavailable in UI when current review/audit/readiness is not present.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 pod-slice: central /api/actions/{action_id}/apply now accepts typed wordpress_draft input (work_item_id, handoff_id, draft_package_id, target_url). apply_action builds a capability from the current WILQ snapshot/content review+audit, binds exact IDs/public canonical URL/confirm actor, then reaches the existing WordPress execution adapter only with live env enabled and dev-host guard. Connector blocks public/arbitrary hosts before HTTP. Focused action mutation, connector write, and content execution tests pass. Bead remains open: next verification must exercise the real capability builder through the route and add dashboard apply CTA only after readiness proof.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 continuation: shared @wilq/shared-schemas now defines ActionWordPressDraftApplyInputSchema and ActionApplyRequestSchema carries wordpress_draft; dashboard applyAction serializes the same typed contract through existing /api/actions/{id}/apply. Real _wordpress_draft_apply_capability builder is covered with controlled current snapshot/review/audit test, including canonical URL mismatch blocker. Shared schema, dashboard API, action mutation, WordPress adapter and content execution suites pass. Handoff: next slice is route-level readiness proof and minimal review-only CTA; do not enable live write CTA before ready_to_request_apply is proven.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 UI continuation: WordPressDraftWorkPanel exposes a conditional review-only CTA only when draft_package_ready && handoff_ready. It links to existing /actions/act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff and explicitly states it performs no write/publication; no /apply call or live-write CTA was added. ContentWorkflowSurface focused Vitest 15/15, dashboard lint/typecheck and browser screenshot pass. Live stale queue remains refresh-first and correctly hides CTA. Next: route-level readiness proof for a truly authorized ActionObject; only then consider any confirm/apply control.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 route proof: added API integration test through /api/actions/act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff/apply using the real _wordpress_draft_apply_capability builder with controlled current snapshot/review/audit dependencies. Exact typed IDs/actor bind, adapter stub is reached only after impact/preview/confirm audit, response is applied=true and mutation_audit.adapter_reached=true. Focused action/content/connector suite passes, Ruff/mypy green. Complexity now records 3 focused test-function budget exceptions (121/105/130 LOC), no production frozen-file growth. Next remaining scope: live readiness proof with a real current review/audit or keep UI apply blocked; no public/live publish path.

title: Fail closed direct WordPress live execution outside ActionObject apply
description:
## Problem
POST /api/content/work-items/wordpress-draft-execution może uruchomić realny adapter create na podstawie samych eventów preview/review/confirm z akcji prepare albo z niepowiązanej akcji. To omija centralny ActionObject apply, pozwala utworzyć nieautoryzowany lub kolejny draft dev i sprawia, że audit mutation-readiness mówi zero vendor writes mimo realnego write.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Wygeneruj preview, review i confirm dla act_prepare_wordpress_existing_draft_update albo prepare-only WordPress handoff. 2. Odczytaj /api/content/wordpress/draft-write-readiness z tym action_id. 3. Endpoint zwraca ready=true i suggested_write_authorization mimo mode=prepare, apply_allowed=false i vendor_write_possible=false. 4. Tę autoryzację może przyjąć content execution mode=live i osiągnąć adapter create.

## Current Evidence
Live existing update ma update_supported=false, a activation packet przechowuje wcześniejszy execution_result created oraz external_write_attempted=true. Kod: wilq/content/workflow/api.py write readiness/authorization i wilq/content/handoff/wordpress_execution.py.

## Out of Scope
Publikacja, destructive update, implementacja existing-draft update i nowa funkcja vendor write.

## Dependencies and Tests
Zależność: rebaseline c9h9.2 i istniejące ActionObject/content contracts. Testy: prepare/wrong-action/rejected review nie daje authorization, live content endpoint nie wywołuje adaptera, dry-run pozostaje dostępny.

## Done
Żadna contentowa ścieżka live nie osiąga vendor adaptera poza kanonicznym ActionObject apply; focused regression dowodzi zero external write.
design: Seam: istniejący content workflow execution/readiness ma przestać być niezależnym mechanizmem write. Najmniejszy slice blokuje mode=live i nie przekazuje create_wordpress_draft_post z content routera, pozostawiając dry-run. Autoryzacja oparta wyłącznie o prefixy eventów nie może odblokowywać vendor call. Późniejszy osobny task przywróci create draft wyłącznie przez apply-capable ActionObject i ActionMutationAudit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mode=live przez content execution zwraca typed blocker i external_write_attempted=false. 2. draft-write-readiness nie zwraca ready/suggested authorization dla prepare ani existing-update action. 3. Dry-run payload i draft-only/publish/destructive guards pozostają bez zmian. 4. Regression tests dowodzą, że create_draft adapter nie został wywołany. 5. Focused pytest, Ruff, mypy i git diff --check przechodzą.
notes: 2026-07-10 completed proof: direct content execution no longer receives a WordPress adapter; live mode returns action_apply_required with external_write_attempted=false; readiness is ready=false, blocked_outside_action_apply, suggested authorization null, publish/destructive false; central mutation summary reports 0 vendor-write candidates. React direct live branches and prepare-write CTA were removed; even adversarial ready=true produces dry_run with null authorization. Verification: backend 765 passed/2 skipped, shared schemas 31 passed/10 skipped, dashboard 137/137, focused content/action 31/31, Ruff/mypy/lint/typecheck/build/security/API/skill smokes green, live HTTP proof green, desktop/mobile/action screenshots current. Full cold content E2E still times out at snapshot and remains c9h9.6, not a write-safety regression.
close_reason: Direct content live execution and UI live branches are fail-closed; live HTTP, full backend/frontend gates and browser proof show zero vendor write outside canonical apply.

title: Re-baseline cleanup graph against current code and product evidence
description: The original audit and several Bead descriptions still cite pre-refactor file sizes, old verification results and completed transitional states. Current evidence differs: schemas are split, tests are separated, API reports 95,644 metric facts/4,469 refresh runs, and the content queue is blocked at 1/3 required actionable candidates. Without reconciliation the graph can drive duplicate or wrongly-prioritized work.
design: Re-run the audit manifests, complexity report, dashboard route usefulness check and focused verification gates. For every open child of c9h9, classify: complete/continue/superseded/split into new issue. Update descriptions and dependencies in place; archive only historical proof. Do not close work based on a green narrow test alone.
acceptance_criteria: A concise current audit ledger names each c9h9 child, current evidence, remaining acceptance gap and owner task. Obsolete tasks are closed or superseded with links; new product blockers are represented in Beads. Complexity, API health/queue and dashboard/browser proof are timestamped. No active task retains an already-completed acceptance criterion as its primary scope.
notes:
2026-07-10 independent rebaseline rebuilt from current git/runtime/API/tests/browser evidence. The current map is docs/audits/2026-07-10-cleanup-rebaseline.md. Confirmed product gaps now have c9h9.3-.6 and r564.2-.3 with dependencies. Keep this graph-reconciliation task open: broad cleanup parents and remaining hotspot scopes still need current descriptions; no duplicate monolith tasks were created.
2026-07-10 final rebaseline update: runtime 95,740 metric facts/4,507 refresh runs, queue blocked 1/2 actionable against minimum 3, connectors 12/9 configured/2 missing credentials/1 disabled. Beads dependency directions corrected and cycle check clean. Added c9h9.7-.13 from observed full-gate failures: ServiceProfile test stability, stale E2E assertions, Ads/custom-segments/actions/knowledge/Merchant cold latency. r564.4 preview-card gap was implemented. Complexity: 33 changed files, one intentional frozen service.py fallback change, 15 existing changed-code budget violations.
close_reason: Current audit ledger is reconciled against ba033433, live WILQ API, complexity, dashboard usefulness/browser proof, focused tests and current Beads child statuses. c9h9.3-.13 are correctly closed; r564.3 remains the explicit active external-state blocker. No completed acceptance scope remains unclassified.

title: Resolve public-page decision before creating a dev draft
description: WILQ has one public production site (ekologus.pl) and one dev WordPress workspace (ekologus.dev.proudsite.pl). For the Ahrefs candidate, WILQ lacks the decision whether it belongs to an existing public page or needs a genuinely new public target URL. The dev site is never an SEO-inventory competitor; it is only the later draft workspace.
design: Build a typed API-owned decision view over public ekologus.pl inventory plus the candidate's evidence. Return: existing public page to refresh/merge, proposed new public target URL requiring human approval, or blocked. After that decision, map the selected public target to a dev draft target for preview-only authoring. Do not compare public and dev as competing canonicals and do not infer a new URL from Ahrefs alone.
acceptance_criteria: The candidate produces one Polish decision card naming either an existing public ekologus.pl URL or an explicitly review-required proposed public URL. It then names the matching dev target only as a draft workspace. Evidence IDs, freshness and human-review gate are present; tests prove dev URLs cannot become canonical SEO targets.
notes: 2026-07-10 correction: the prior wording incorrectly implied a broad multi-site duplicate resolver. Scope is public ekologus.pl decision first, then dev.proudsite.pl draft mapping only.
close_reason: Redundant: public/dev URL and canonical guards already exist; live Ahrefs candidate lacks a business-approved page target, not canonical infrastructure.

title: Extract Merchant content analytics local SEO and command schemas
description: Extract the remaining Merchant, Content, GA4, Localo, Ahrefs and Command Center/Daily Check schema groups from wilq/schemas/__init__.py into explicit sibling modules. Preserve the compatibility facade, public imports and current content-workflow route label; do not change product behavior or workflow call sites.
design: Use dependencies: merchant -> core/marketing/actions; content -> core/marketing; ga4/localo -> core/marketing; ahrefs -> core/content; command_center -> core/actions/marketing/ads. Sibling modules never import wilq.schemas. Keep helper labels and WorkOrder/DailyCheck logic with command_center. Re-export all modules through the facade and preserve Pydantic model names/OpenAPI.
acceptance_criteria: Remaining domain models no longer live in schemas/__init__.py except explicit facade imports; facade becomes a thin compatibility export map; public from wilq.schemas imports and OpenAPI remain valid; no sibling facade imports; full contract suite, ruff, mypy, import-cycle scan and live API/OpenAPI checks pass; current /content-workflow route label remains Treści.
close_reason: Extracted remaining Merchant, Content, GA4, Localo, Ahrefs and Command Center schema domains; full contracts, Ruff, mypy, import scan, live health and OpenAPI passed.

title: Extract Ads and Demand Gen schema domains
description: Extract the Ads and Demand Gen Pydantic contract cluster from wilq/schemas/__init__.py into wilq/schemas/ads.py. Preserve existing public wilq.schemas imports and OpenAPI names; the remaining Merchant, content and analytics models must not be changed in this slice.
design: Ads depends on core, actions and marketing contracts. Import sibling modules directly and never import the compatibility facade. Keep action preview/review types and ActionReviewOutcome as explicit dependencies. Preserve all Ads label helpers and helper functions in the new module; re-export the module through wilq.schemas.
acceptance_criteria: Ads/Demand Gen classes and helper functions live in ads.py; facade non-empty LOC drops materially; no schema sibling imports wilq.schemas; Ads/vendor-read, Ads action and command-center contract suites pass; ruff/mypy/import/OpenAPI checks pass; public model names and current operator labels remain unchanged.
close_reason: Extracted Ads and Demand Gen schema domains; focused and full contracts, Ruff, mypy, import scan, live health and OpenAPI passed.

title: Extract action knowledge and marketing schema domains
description: Extract the ActionObject/action-review, knowledge-card/expert, and marketing-brief/tactical-queue model groups from wilq/schemas/__init__.py into sibling domain modules. Keep wilq.schemas as the public compatibility facade, preserve the current /content-workflow route label, and do not migrate content workflow call sites.
design: Use a one-way dependency graph: core -> actions, knowledge, marketing; the remaining Ads/Merchant/Content/GA4/Localo/Ahrefs/Command Center models consume explicit sibling imports from core/actions/marketing and never import the facade. title: Split wilq/schemas package into domain schema modules
description: wilq/schemas/__init__.py is the frozen compatibility module after wilq-seo-b4kg.1 extracted a 522-line shared core. The remaining 4484 non-empty LOC still mix ActionObject, knowledge, marketing, Ads, Merchant, content, analytics, local and command-center contracts. Further extraction must preserve public from wilq.schemas imports and avoid Pydantic import cycles.
design: Keep wilq/schemas/__init__.py as the stable public facade. New sibling domain modules import only lower-level siblings such as core, never wilq.schemas. Continue in dependency order: actions, knowledge and marketing before Ads/content. Preserve model names, OpenAPI components and current content-workflow route label; do not migrate content workflow call sites during a generic schema split.
acceptance_criteria: New behavior-driving fields land in domain modules, not the frozen facade. wilq/schemas/__init__.py becomes a thin compatibility export map or documented small core. Existing API contracts/OpenAPI and public imports pass; ruff/mypy pass for touched modules; no import cycles appear.
close_reason: Split wilq.schemas into explicit domain modules behind a 12-line compatibility facade; public imports and API contracts remain green.

title: Dismantle tests/test_api_contracts.py mega-test
description: tests/test_api_contracts.py is 21,233 lines / 20,109 non-empty LOC and contains many 100-2914 line tests. It mixes credential redaction, Ads vendor reads, GA4, Merchant, Localo, Ahrefs, command center, content diagnostics and action previews. This is test theater risk: huge fixtures and implementation assertions make refactors harder without giving focused product confidence.
design: Split by behavior domain into small files under tests/api_contracts or existing tests/content/tests/connectors: security redaction, actions, Ads, Merchant, GA4, Localo, Ahrefs, command center, content workflow. Extract shared seed builders into named fixtures/factories. Replace giant tests with smaller behavior/usefulness assertions: evidence IDs/source connectors, blocked unsafe claims, freshness, ActionObject safety, Polish operator labels where relevant. Delete assertions that only preserve obsolete route strings or raw implementation wording.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_api_contracts.py is reduced below the changed-file budget or becomes a thin compatibility index with no giant test bodies. Largest extracted test functions stay under the complexity budget unless documented. Focused pytest subsets pass for each domain and coverage still catches missing evidence/source connectors/unsafe write regressions.

title: Replace apps/dashboard/src/routes/App.test.tsx monolith
description: apps/dashboard/src/routes/App.test.tsx is 9,205 lines and preserves broad dashboard behavior through one massive test file. It risks snapshot-like confidence and route-string preservation instead of product usefulness. Current route cleanup already deletes ContentDiagnosticSurface/content-planner tests, so the remaining dashboard tests must not keep obsolete IA alive.
design: Split App.test.tsx into route-focused behavior tests and shared test utilities: Shell/navigation, command-center, content-workflow, actions/action-detail, settings/source refresh, diagnostic surfaces. Prefer queries that prove the first-screen decision/blocker/next safe step, not long strings or raw payload mechanics. Remove tests that keep /content-planner or hidden placeholder surfaces as primary marketer behavior.
acceptance_criteria: App.test.tsx no longer acts as the dashboard omnibus. Tests are domain files with clear behavior names, no obsolete primary /content-planner assertions, and dashboard test/typecheck still pass. The test suite proves marketer-mode first-screen behavior and technical details below the fold.

title: Freeze gate: stop growing frozen monolith files
description: Current complexity audit reports 50 changed files, 77 changed-code budget violations, and frozen-growth changes in tests/test_api_contracts.py plus wilq/schemas.py. This violates the repo's own anti-slop standard and makes any senior-grade cleanup harder.
design: Before new product behavior lands, require changed slices to move behavior out of frozen files or explicitly split them. Keep scripts/audit_complexity.py as gate; do not silence it broadly. The first enforcement target is to remove current changes from tests/test_api_contracts.py and wilq/schemas.py by extracting domain test files/schema modules or documenting a narrow extraction slice.
acceptance_criteria: rtk uv run python scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed passes without --allow-frozen and without changed-code budget violations, or a documented extraction PR reduces frozen LOC and isolates unavoidable temporary violations. No new behavior is added to tests/test_api_contracts.py or wilq/schemas.py.
close_reason: Closed the freeze-gate classification slice. Current frozen diffs are documented route-compat changes only (/content-planner -> /content-workflow), focused Python tests and dashboard tests/typecheck pass, docs/CONTEXT.md now requires focused child beads for any temporary frozen-file exception. The actual monolith reductions remain tracked separately by wilq-seo-50wa and wilq-seo-b4kg.

title: Split ContentWorkflowSurface into real React boundaries
description: apps/dashboard/src/routes/ContentWorkflowSurface.tsx is 3,543 lines. It mixes data selection, API response interpretation, UI layout, editing state, WordPress/ACF preview, claim/proof panels, copy and legacy detail mechanics. This violates the audit target and React docs guidance around pure components, local state, avoiding unnecessary effects and deriving values during render instead of synchronizing redundant state.
design: Keep the route as orchestration only. Extract explicit components/hooks: ContentWorkbenchHeader, SourceStatusStrip, WorkItemSelector, PublicPageSectionMap, DevTargetSelector, AcfCurrentVsProposedPanel, DraftSectionEditor, ProofClaimSummary, LegacyDetailsDisclosure, useContentWorkflowData, useDraftSectionOverrides. Business meaning must remain API-owned; React components render typed fields and local form state only. Use useMemo only for expensive derived lists, not as a band-aid for impure render.
acceptance_criteria: ContentWorkflowSurface.tsx falls under 800 LOC or a documented route-shell budget, with extracted components under stable size limits. No fixed ACF field-name assumptions are introduced. Tests prove public-vs-dev target selection, current vs proposed ACF values, edited section draft-only execution, and marketer first-screen decision without lower legacy panels dominating.

title: Full repo senior audit and anti-slop cleanup plan
description: User requested a harsh full-repository senior audit based on docs/audits/2026-07-08-full-repo-senior-code-audit-prompt.md and the pasted audit prompt. The work must map every repo file, prove coverage, identify implementation/test/docs/dashboard/API/skills architecture slop, then turn it into precise cleanup Beads with dependencies. Evidence already gathered: .local-lab/audits/full-repo-senior-code-audit-20260708/manifest.jsonl maps 647 files, 633 text files read for manifest hash/line count, 11 binary/asset files metadata-inspected, 3 deleted worktree files explicitly skipped. Complexity proof shows 77 changed-code budget violations and frozen-growth changes in tests/test_api_contracts.py and wilq/schemas.py.
design: Do not fix code under this epic until audit proof and issue graph exist. Use WILQ product rules: API owns product logic, React renders typed view-models, skills consume API, tests prove behavior/usefulness not dead UI. Compare dashboard React against official React guidance: pure render, derived values during render/useMemo when expensive, avoid unnecessary Effects, local state only where needed, split route-level components/hooks. Compare Python against typed domain contracts, small modules, explicit public API and behavior-focused tests.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when the final audit has FILE_COVERAGE_PROOF, top findings, test theater audit, dashboard/API/Python/skills/docs audits, delete/quarantine list, split/refactor list, senior rebuild phases, first PR recommendation, strict scores, and all resulting implementation cleanup is represented as Beads with dependencies and verification gates.

title: Redesign single action detail into marketer decision screen
description: The current /actions/:actionId view is overloaded with technical conditions, blocked claims and raw review mechanics. For a marketer, opening act_review_merchant_feed_issues should answer: what is this action checking, why it matters, whether any write/apply is allowed, what evidence supports it, and what the next safe click is. Technical payload, ActionObject internals, audit trail and blocked-claim mechanics must move below the fold or into technical details.
design: Build a decision-first action detail layout: hero summary, write status (preview/review/confirm/audit), primary next safe step, short evidence/proof summary, exact blockers in normal Polish, and a compact action timeline. Keep ActionObject validation/preview/review/confirm mechanics intact but do not force the operator to decode raw conditions before acting. Start with merchant feed issue action act_review_merchant_feed_issues and reuse the pattern for other actions.
acceptance_criteria: Opening /actions/act_review_merchant_feed_issues gives a clear first-screen answer in 30 seconds: what to do, why, whether write is blocked, and what evidence exists. No raw payload/contract/technical IDs appear above the fold unless they directly block action. Tests cover the marketer-first labels and that write remains blocked without review/confirmation.
close_reason: Decision-first action detail is implemented and covered by ActionDetailRoute tests: Polish next-step hero, write-blocked status, evidence summary, blocker labels, and technical payload hidden below fold.

title: Add dashboard auto-refresh job loop for stale sources
description: Dashboard should not rely on manual refresh rituals. When a source needed by the current marketer surface is stale, WILQ should expose and/or trigger a safe read-only refresh job, show queued/running/partial/failed/ready state, invalidate the relevant API view-model after completion, and update the decision queue without requiring Codex commands. Writes and vendor mutations remain behind ActionObject review; this task is only for read-only source refresh and job status.
design: Use WILQ API as canonical brain. Add a typed source-refresh/job status contract consumable by dashboard surfaces: source_id, freshness, refresh_allowed, last_run, current_run, status_label, safe_next_step, affected_decisions. Dashboard should show 'odświeżam', 'odświeżone', 'nie udało się' and 'częściowy odczyt' states. Start with Merchant/GA4 because stale data currently blocks useful decisions. Do not hardcode stale copy in React; copy must follow API freshness/job state.
acceptance_criteria: On /merchant or /sources, stale read-only sources can be refreshed through a dashboard/API flow without shell commands. The UI shows job progress and result, clears stale warnings when API returns requires_refresh=false, and keeps stale/partial/failed states honest. Tests prove the stale banner is conditional on API state and that read refresh does not create write-capable actions or bypass ActionObject safety.
notes: 2026-07-07 first slice: /settings now exposes a read-only vendor_refresh button for configured stale daily sources via POST /api/connectors/{connector}/refresh. The UI shows refreshing/success/error states and invalidates source/diagnostic/command-center query caches after success. Verified with App.test.tsx focused test, dashboard typecheck, dashboard lint and git diff --check. Remaining: richer queued/running/partial job status and automatic refresh loop on source-dependent surfaces.

description: Treści/SEO work orders now expose concrete URL and WordPress title/H1, but still do not expose current H2, ACF Flexible Content rows/sections, or a safe current-content summary. Add a read-only WordPress section inventory contract: source URL/object ID, title/H1, modified date, section headings/H2, ACF flexible field/layout names when available, excerpt/short summary, evidence ID and freshness. The dashboard should show these fields in /content-planner and use missing-section-inventory as a blocker instead of asking the marketer to rewrite from a query.
acceptance_criteria: For a matched WordPress content decision, /api/content/diagnostics exposes current section inventory or an explicit missing contract with next step. /content-planner shows concrete article/page, title/H1, modified date, current sections/H2/ACF rows when available, GSC queries, missing sections/unknowns and the next safe step. No final rewrite or publish claim is shown without workflow/review.

close_reason: Public WordPress H2/H3 section inventory is exposed in content decisions and dashboard; missing ACF rows are explicit blocker/missing contract.

title: Make Content SEO queue concrete with WordPress article and ACF context
description: Widok Treści i SEO oraz kolejka contentowa nie mogą pokazywać abstraktów typu 'odśwież lub scal ekologus (33 zapytań)' bez odpowiedzi: która strona/post WordPress, jaki publiczny URL, jaki tytuł/H1, jakie aktualne sekcje/H2/ACF flexible content, jaka aktualna treść/skrót, które zapytania GSC do niej prowadzą, czego brakuje i co dokładnie zmienić. Przebudować content queue na realne work orders oparte o WordPress inventory + GSC + ACF/sekcje. GA4 measurement blockers nie mogą być zadaniami contentowymi; mają być routingiem/blokadą do Reklamy i pomiar. Acceptance: marketer po wejściu w Treści i SEO widzi konkretne artykuły/podstrony do ogarnięcia, ich aktualny stan WordPress/ACF, brakujące sekcje, źródła zapytań i jasny next step. Nie ma pozycji 'ekologus' bez kontekstu ani 'problem pomiaru, nie treści' jako content task.
design: Parent outcome: one API-owned content workbench for a concrete public URL with public sections, current source signals, dev draft context and a safe next step. Product semantics are implemented in focused children: freshness, latency, canonical apply, duplicate-draft reuse and mobile first viewport. React ho41 handles component boundaries only and must not invent decision rules.
acceptance_criteria: Parent closes only when the selected work item carries current evidence and freshness, public and dev roles remain distinct, an existing dev draft is reused instead of duplicated, any real create goes through exact ActionObject apply and audit, desktop and mobile show decision/blocker/CTA, and focused API/UI/browser proof passes.

title: Redesign action detail as operator decision screen
description: The current /actions/{actionId} detail is too technical for marketers: it exposes raw conditions, blocked claims, payload-style details and audit mechanics before telling the operator what to do. Redesign action detail as a decision screen: what this action will do, current safety state, preview/draft/review/confirm steps, required human decision, blockers, evidence summary and audit drawer below the fold.
design: First viewport: action title in Polish, status chip, one recommended operator decision, impact/scope summary, safe next CTA, blocked write explanation if applicable. Technical details such as ActionObject, payload, evidence IDs, validation conditions, raw blockers and audit events move to collapsible audit/details. For marketer mode, 'czego nie wolno twierdzić' appears only when it directly affects this action, written as plain risk language.
acceptance_criteria: A marketer opening /actions/{actionId} understands within 30 seconds whether to preview, review, confirm, or stop. Raw IDs/payloads/contracts are not first-level UI. The screen preserves safety: no write/apply without validation, preview, human review, confirmation and audit.
close_reason: Rebuilt /actions/{actionId} as an operator decision screen: first viewport now shows decision, safety state, next CTA and write blockers; raw payload/apply/audit details moved below fold.

title: Rebuild /command-center as Dzisiaj mockup 1:1
description: Implement the Dzisiaj screen from the provided mockup as the primary daily cockpit. It must show stat tiles, source freshness strip, next best work, blockers that must not be bypassed, forbidden claims, daily queue and quick filters. This replaces the current 'Centrum pracy' card-heavy route.
design: Use WorkOrder API data as the main model. First viewport: title 'Dzisiaj', date/refresh controls, four summary tiles, source freshness strip, two-column next-best-work and blockers panel, then compact 'Kolejka dziś' table. Use marketer-readable Polish copy and no raw evidence/action IDs on first viewport.
acceptance_criteria: Route /command-center visually matches Image #1 intent and structure. In 30 seconds Wilku can identify top work, why, blockers, forbidden claims and next safe action. Existing evidence/action/freshness truth remains accessible below fold/details. Screenshot and usefulness audit recorded.
notes: Visual reference: docs/audits/dashboard-visualization/01-dzisiaj-command-center.png. Match this as the 1:1 target for /command-center / Dzisiaj.
close_reason: Implemented Dzisiaj /command-center mockup viewport with WorkOrder queue, source freshness strip, next-best work, blockers, forbidden claims and dense daily table. Verified CommandCenterRoute test, focused App route contract, dashboard primitives tests, typecheck and diff check.

title: Implement shared mockup-grade dashboard shell and visual primitives
description: Build the shared layout primitives needed to reproduce the provided target mockups 1:1: compact left navigation, top date/refresh controls, stat tiles, source freshness strips, priority badges, blocker panels, dense tables, status pills and safe CTA patterns. This replaces current generic card-heavy surfaces with one consistent marketer-mode visual system.
design: Create/adjust shared React components and CSS tokens rather than one-off screen markup. Keep cards at <=8px radius, quiet white surface, thin borders, compact spacing, dense tables, icons from lucide or existing logo assets. Technical details remain available only behind System/Audit/details.
acceptance_criteria: A screen can be assembled to match the supplied mockups without bespoke CSS per route. Components support Dzisiaj, Kolejka, Treści i SEO, Reklamy i pomiar, Akcje, Wiedza, Źródła and System patterns. Visual regression screenshots show no nested card spam and no raw technical IDs on first viewport.
notes: Visual references: docs/audits/dashboard-visualization/README.md plus all PNG targets in that directory. This bead owns the shared visual primitives needed to reproduce the mockups, not one-off route styling.
close_reason: Added reusable mockup-grade dashboard primitives: toolbar, compact stat tile, source freshness strip, blocker panel, forbidden claims strip, dense queue table, priority/status/risk pills and action lifecycle strip. Component tests, dashboard typecheck and focused route tests pass.

title: Redesign /content-workflow as stepper with one active decision
description: Second opinion calls /content-workflow the strongest product screen and the most overloaded. It needs a guided stepper: preflight, plan/claims, brief, draft WP, review, publish/apply.
design: First viewport shows work title/status/owner, stepper, one active decision, blockers, forbidden claims and primary CTA. Claim ledger, ACF mapping, WP payload, evidence IDs and ActionObject details move to tabs/drawers.
acceptance_criteria: Content workflow first screen has one clear operator decision and one next safe step. Publish/write remains blocked without review. Claim ledger summary stays visible, raw details are below fold.
close_reason: Redesigned /content-workflow first viewport into a guided stepper with one active operator decision, publication blockers, do-not-claim list, claim-ledger summary and anchors to details below. Tests now assert old overloaded first panel is gone, technical controls are below the fold, and safety/WordPress gates remain visible. Verified focused test, dashboard route subset, typecheck and git diff check.

title: Redesign /command-center as one daily work queue
description: Second opinion says /command-center has the right intent but becomes four mini dashboards. It should answer what Wilku should do now, what is blocked, what not to promise, and what is next.
design: First viewport: header with data health summary, one 'następna najlepsza praca' panel, blockers/do-not-claim panel, then a compact queue table/list. Drilldowns open domain views; no equal-weight card spam.
acceptance_criteria: In 30 seconds, a marketer can identify the top work item, why it matters, blockers, forbidden claims and next safe action without reading raw IDs. Screenshot audit compares before/after and records usefulness score.
notes: Completed /command-center first-viewport tightening. Live stack: API http://127.0.0.1:8000 ok, dashboard http://127.0.0.1:5175/command-center ok. Changes: top KPI now counts active work orders instead of review-only orders, duplicate bottom ForbiddenClaimsStrip removed so forbidden claims stay in the blocker panel. Proof: typecheck passed, CommandCenterRoute.test + App.test passed, git diff check passed, screenshot /tmp/wilq-command-after.png shows 4 decisions, 4 blockers, next-best work, blocker panel and compact daily queue.
close_reason: Done: /command-center first viewport is one daily work queue with correct active-work count and no duplicate forbidden-claims strip.

title: Introduce Work Order model for dashboard decisions
description: The UI needs work orders instead of equal-weight diagnostic cards. A decision should carry what to do, owner, status, evidence, blocked claims, next safe step and completion condition.
design: Add a small API/view-model contract that can wrap existing DailyDecision/ActionObject/opportunity rows without rewriting all diagnostics. Include owner_role, status, priority, source connectors, evidence summary, blockers, forbidden claims, next_safe_step and close_condition.
acceptance_criteria: At least Command Center can render a work-order-shaped queue from live API state. Work orders are marketer-readable in Polish and keep technical IDs available only in details.
close_reason: Added WorkOrder backend/shared/frontend contract, filled Command Center work_orders from DailyDecision, rendered Command Center from work_orders with close conditions and marketer-readable fields, and verified backend/shared/dashboard tests.

title: Hide placeholder and technical routes from marketer primary nav
description: The second opinion calls placeholder Ads subroutes and technical routes trust-killers. Search terms, scaling, seasonality, recommendations and weak custom segment surfaces must not appear as full primary screens until they have real queues.
design: Use route status from the IA registry. Placeholder routes may remain directly routable for development, but primary marketer nav must hide them or show them only inside technical/audit mode with clear status and repair path.
acceptance_criteria: Marketer nav no longer exposes empty/placeholder routes. Each hidden route has explicit status and, if opened directly, a concise explanation of what data/action is missing and where the real workflow lives.
close_reason: Primary nav is registry-driven and excludes placeholder/experimental/technical surfaces; hidden Ads placeholder direct routes now render compact readiness blockers with safe route back to /ads-doctor instead of generic registry dumps. Verified with surfaceRegistry/GenericSurface/App route tests, dashboard typecheck and screenshots.

title: Define marketer-mode IA and technical/audit mode boundary
description: Create the target information architecture from the 2nd opinion: Dzisiaj, Kolejka, Treści i SEO, Reklamy i pomiar, Produkty, Lokalnie, Akcje as marketer mode; Źródła, Wiedza and Audyt systemu as secondary/admin.
design: Implement as product-facing route/nav registry or docs-backed design decision before broad UI edits. Each route gets mode, visibility, production/experimental/placeholder status, owner persona and first-screen decision intent.
acceptance_criteria: One typed route/nav registry or equivalent contract can derive primary nav and technical/admin nav. Existing primary routes Procesy, Szanse, Baza wiedzy and Ustawienia are reclassified or renamed according to marketer/admin mode. Tests assert placeholders are not primary marketer nav.
close_reason: Implemented typed marketer/admin/technical surface IA. Primary nav now derives from registry as Dzisiaj, Kolejka, Treści i SEO, Reklamy i pomiar, Produkty, Lokalnie, Akcje; secondary System nav exposes Procesy, GA4, Wiedza and Źródła. Tests assert placeholders/technical routes stay out of primary marketer nav.

title: Dashboard marketer-mode redesign from 2nd opinion
description: Second opinion on 2026-07-07 dashboard screenshots says WILQ product direction is good, but UI must be aggressively cut down. The dashboard currently exposes system internals and card spam instead of guiding Wilku through today's work.
design: Pivot UI from mechanism showcase to marketer operating cockpit. Primary mode shows what to do now, why, blockers, what not to claim, data freshness and next safe action. Technical/audit mode keeps evidence IDs, ActionObject, runtime, contracts and logs below the fold. Do not remove WILQ safety mechanisms; remove visual noise and route confusion.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard primary IA supports marketer mode with a clear daily cockpit, decision/action queue, content/SEO workflow, ads+measurement, products and local drilldowns. Placeholder/technical routes are removed from primary nav. Command Center and Content Workflow are redesigned around work orders/active steps. Screens are re-audited with screenshot packet and usefulness score before close.

title: Add slop-killing tests for daily-check recommendations
description: Current tests/evals can prove screens render and skills preserve evidence, but audit 006 requires tests that kill unsupported operator recommendations before they reach Wilku.
design: Add deterministic tests around DailyCheckResult/ExpertRule output: no recommendation without evidence, no recommendation without expert rule, stale connector blocks conclusion, Merchant issue without severity/destination does not become fix-now, GA4 missing key events blocks performance conclusion, Ads missing conversions blocks CPA/ROAS, GSC movement without baseline blocks spadek claim, WordPress staging URL cannot become SEO evidence, skill output cannot bypass API contract.
acceptance_criteria: The test suite fails on unsupported daily recommendations and passes when the API returns typed blockers with evidence/freshness/rule context.

title: Implement /wilq-daily-check workflow v0
description: Command Center is useful, but audit 006 says WILQ still lacks a BDOS-style one-command daily operating workflow powered by expert rules. This task creates the first API-owned daily check output for Ekologus.
design: Use existing connector evidence and ExpertRules to compile a Polish operator-ready daily check: GSC page/query movement, GA4 traffic/measurement sanity, WordPress inventory/canonical state, Merchant issue summary, Ads only when evidence supports it, and Ahrefs/Localo as supporting evidence. Missing proof becomes a blocker.
acceptance_criteria: API returns a DailyCheckResult or equivalent endpoint with evidence IDs, source connectors, expert rule IDs, freshness, blockers, confidence, safe next actions and do-not-touch guidance. The output answers what Wilku should check today without reading raw diagnostics.
close_reason: Added API-owned /api/marketing/daily-check over existing runtime with typed DailyCheckResult, evidence/source/rule/freshness traceability, fail-closed stale blockers and do-not-touch safety; verified tests, live API and browser proof.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Wykonano pierwszy API-owned daily-check projection: nowy typed GET /api/marketing/daily-check kompiluje istniejący build_daily_runtime do DailyCheckResult. Wynik zawiera checked/skipped connectors, freshness, evidence IDs, source connectors, expert rule IDs, blocked recommendations, safe next actions i do_not_touch; stale/missing trace jest fail-closed. Focused daily API/schema tests, existing expert/diagnostic contracts, Ruff, mypy, complexity (0 changed-code violations) i diff check green. Live po restarcie: status=blocked, 9 checked / 3 skipped, freshness=stale, 21 evidence IDs, 8 expert rule IDs, 1 do_not_touch. Browser proof .local-lab/proof/daily-check-command-center.png. Następny slice: v9ab.8 false-positive guards.

title: Define DailyCheckResult output contract
description: Daily check needs a strict product contract, not loose dashboard copy. Audit 006 names DailyCheckResult as the shape that should carry checked connectors, skipped connectors, anomalies, risks, opportunities, blocked recommendations and safe next actions.
design: Add a typed DailyCheckResult schema/API model with workspace_id, date, status, checked_connectors, skipped_connectors, anomalies, risks, opportunities, blocked_recommendations, safe_next_actions, do_not_touch, evidence, expert_rules_used and freshness.
acceptance_criteria: Schema/API contract tests fail if a recommendation/action lacks evidence IDs, source connectors, freshness or expert rule references where required.
close_reason: Added DailyCheckResult contract and mirrored shared Zod schema. The contract separates checked/skipped connectors, anomalies, risks, opportunities, blocked recommendations, safe next actions and do-not-touch items. DailyCheckItem now requires source connectors, evidence IDs, expert rule IDs and non-unknown freshness for non-blocked operator recommendations. Verified focused Python contract tests, shared schema tests and dashboard typecheck.

title: Implement ExpertRule contract and first real rules
description: WILQ recommendations need to bind condition, required connectors/metrics/windows, false-positive checks, blocked states, source IDs and safe next actions. Without this, the system can be useful-looking but not BDOS-class.
design: Add ExpertRule as a typed API/domain contract. Include domain, rule_id, condition, required_connectors, required_metrics, minimum_window, segmentation, false_positive_checks, blocked_states, recommendation_template, forbidden_conclusions, safety_level, source_ids and eval_case_ids. Seed at least one real Merchant or GA4 rule and one blocker rule.
acceptance_criteria: At least two concrete ExpertRules exist with source IDs and tests; one API diagnostic/daily-check path can reference rule IDs instead of only prose.
close_reason: Added the complete typed ExpertRule/ExpertRuleSummary contract, populated five real source-backed rules, wired existing diagnostics to rule IDs, and verified tests, live API and browser proof.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Pierwszy zakres wykonany: ExpertRule i ExpertRuleSummary dostały typed condition, required_connectors, required_metrics, minimum_window, segmentation, false_positive_checks, blocked_states, recommendation_template, forbidden_conclusions, safety_level i eval_case_ids. Pięć realnych platform-trap YAML packs wypełnia kontrakt i zachowuje source IDs; Ads/GA4/Merchant diagnostics referencują rule IDs. Tests: platform trap + pełne expert/daily/Ads/GA4/Merchant/source suites, Ruff, mypy, complexity (0 changed-code violations) i diff check green. Live po restarcie: health ok, 98,915 facts / 4,572 refresh runs; API zwraca pełne pola. Browser proof: .local-lab/proof/v9ab5-expertrule-merchant-viewport.png. Następny slice po zamknięciu: v9ab.7 daily-check workflow.

title: Add official platform trap rule packs
description: Audit 006 calls out that BDOS encodes API/platform traps in code. WILQ needs source-backed platform rule packs for Google Ads API, GA4, Merchant, GSC and WordPress so diagnostics do not rely on skill prose or memory.
design: Create minimal rule-pack structures that cite ExpertKnowledgeSource IDs and encode specific traps: GAQL/query/mutation constraints, GA4 attribution/measurement caveats, Merchant status/issue caveats, GSC baseline/data limits, WordPress canonical/inventory checks.
acceptance_criteria: At least one rule pack per priority platform exists with source IDs and tests; diagnostics or daily-check candidates can reference platform rule IDs when blocking or explaining recommendations.
close_reason: Added five source-backed typed platform trap packs, exposed them through existing expert API/source registry, wired Ads/GA4/Merchant diagnostics to rule IDs, and verified contracts, live API and browser proof.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Wykonano platform-trap rule packs: typed PlatformTrapContract oraz ads_platform_traps_v1, ga4_platform_traps_v1, merchant_platform_traps_v1, gsc_platform_traps_v1 i wordpress_platform_traps_v1. Istniejące /api/expert/rules, rule-summaries i sources zachowują granicę API; Ads/GA4/Merchant diagnostics referencują nowe IDs. Live proof po managed restart: health ok, 98,915 metric facts / 4,572 refresh runs, pięć trap contracts z source IDs i safe next steps; Merchant queue zawiera merchant_platform_traps_v1. Focused/full contracts, Ruff, mypy, complexity i diff check przechodzą; browser proof .local-lab/proof/platform-trap-merchant-first-viewport.png. Następny odblokowany slice: v9ab.5 ExpertRule contract.

title: Add ExpertKnowledgeSource registry
description: WILQ needs a structured source registry for expert/platform/operator knowledge. Raw docs, skill prose, public platform docs or internal SOPs should not be used as loose prompt stuffing.
design: Introduce ExpertKnowledgeSource with domain, source_type, license_status, source_reference, freshness_date, reviewer, trust_level and allowed_usage. Seed only safe examples from official docs or internal reviewed references; do not ingest raw paid-course material.
acceptance_criteria: Registry model/schema exists, has seed examples with source IDs, and tests prove recommendations/rules can cite source IDs without exposing raw private material.
close_reason: Implemented ExpertKnowledgeSource registry with API endpoint, source_ids on rules/summaries, shared schema parity, tests, and live endpoint proof.

title: Define WILQ expert knowledge taxonomy
description: Current knowledge mixes Ekologus service facts with expert operating logic. Audit 006 requires a typed taxonomy before WILQ can become BDOS-class: client truth, expert operating knowledge, platform/API trap knowledge, workspace memory and observed outcome knowledge.
design: Add or update API/domain contracts so service facts cannot masquerade as expert rules. Keep the taxonomy small and code-consumable; avoid prompt-only definitions.
acceptance_criteria: Typed schema/contracts and focused tests show ClientTruthKnowledge, ExpertOperatingKnowledge, PlatformTrapKnowledge, WorkspaceMemory and ObservedOutcomeKnowledge are distinct concepts with clear allowed usage.
close_reason: Implemented API-owned knowledge taxonomy with five distinct types: client_truth, expert_operating, platform_trap, workspace_memory and observed_outcome. Added /api/knowledge/taxonomy, backend correlation guard, shared Zod schema/correlation guard and focused Python/TS tests. Verified live endpoint after local stack restart.

title: Align BDOS maturity wording with audit 006
description: Audit 006 says the defensible 35-45% number is platform substrate maturity, not BDOS workflow parity or real daily operator usefulness. Current docs/progress must not imply WILQ is already a better BDOS as a complete operating product.
design: Review the durable progress/roadmap docs and replace broad maturity claims with three separate lanes: platform substrate, BDOS workflow parity and real operator usefulness. Keep this as product truth, not marketing spin.
acceptance_criteria: Docs distinguish substrate maturity from workflow parity and operator usefulness; no durable doc claims full Better-BDOS/product readiness without the audit 006 caveat.
close_reason: Durable roadmap and plan docs now separate substrate maturity, workflow parity and real operator usefulness, with no full-product readiness overclaim.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11 docs proof: PLANS.md and docs/roadmap/bdos-class-wilq-master-roadmap.md now separate platform/substrate maturity (35-45%), BDOS workflow parity (15-25%) and real operator usefulness proof (10-20%). Any 35-45 shorthand is explicitly labeled substrate planning, not product readiness; docs state WILQ is not yet a finished/better BDOS product.

title: Goal 006: Expert Operating Knowledge Kernel + /wilq-daily-check v0
description: Audit 006 says WILQ has useful platform substrate and improved dashboard surfaces, but it is not yet BDOS-class as an operating workflow. This epic turns the next step into concrete product work: API-owned expert rules, platform trap knowledge, a typed daily-check result, usefulness/eval proof and a Wilku review packet after real output exists.
design: Keep WILQ API as the product brain. Skills and dashboard consume API-owned rules and DailyCheckResult; they do not invent product behavior. Focus on a narrow v0 daily operating workflow for Ekologus using existing connectors and evidence, not broad RAG, vendor writes, PDF reporting or social publishing.
acceptance_criteria: Goal 006 is complete when WILQ has typed expert/source/rule contracts, at least one real rule pack used by API output, /wilq-daily-check or equivalent API-owned DailyCheckResult with evidence IDs/source connectors/freshness/rule IDs/blockers/safe next actions, slop-killing tests, a skill eval over the daily-check output, and a Wilku review packet based on one real output.

title: Goal 005: align Wilku service-profile handoff trace labels
description: Make the main Wilku Service Profile handoff use the same private trace gate wording as the live session card and completion guard.
acceptance_criteria: Handoff private trace section uses bramka labels, avoids eval/raw-private/finalny-content wording, and focused tests pass.
close_reason: Wilku handoff private trace labels aligned with live card/completion guard and verified.

title: Goal 005: polish session card content queue wording
description: Replace mixed content-language in the Goal 005 Wilku session card status section with Polish operator wording.
acceptance_criteria: Session card says decyzja treściowa and kolejka treści, no visible Decyzja contentowa/kolejki content in the status section, focused tests and live smoke pass.
close_reason: Session card content queue wording is Polish and verified focused/live.

title: Goal 005: clarify content candidate versus Service Profile review
description: Explain in the Wilku session card that the selected content candidate and first Service Profile review card are two different checks in one UAT session.
acceptance_criteria: Session card status section explains the relationship without exposing technical IDs; focused tests and live smoke pass.
close_reason: Session card now clarifies content candidate vs Service Profile review and focused/live checks pass.

title: Goal 005: align completion guard private trace gate label
description: Use the same bramka: label in Goal 005 completion guard next-UAT private trace output that the session card uses.
acceptance_criteria: Completion guard markdown private trace section shows bramka labels and no eval: label; focused tests, ruff, diff check and live smoke pass.
close_reason: Completion guard private trace label aligned with session card and verified focused/live.

title: Goal 005: polish private trace gate label
description: Replace visible eval: labels in the Wilku Goal 005 private source trace with bramka: while preserving stable goal_* IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Session card private source trace section shows bramka labels, contains no eval: label in the visible trace section, focused tests and live smoke pass.
close_reason: Private trace gate label is Polish in the Wilku session card and verified with focused/live checks.

title: Goal 005: polish UAT verdict proof wording
description: Remove English completion-proof wording from the Wilku-facing Goal 005 session card verdict section.
acceptance_criteria: Session card verdict section uses Polish wording for follow-up/proof, focused tests pass, live generated card contains no completion proof phrase.
close_reason: Polished Wilku-facing verdict proof wording and verified focused/live output.

title: Goal 005: show explicit UAT verdict options in session card
description: Make the Wilku session card explain the exact werdykt_po_15_minutach options and which verdict can unlock Goal 005 completion.
acceptance_criteria: Session card shows all verdict options before technical IDs, explains only the full-test verdict can unlock completion, and focused UAT tests pass.
close_reason: Session card now lists exact UAT verdict options and focused/live checks pass.

title: Goal 005: require ready UAT session verdict for completion
description: Make Goal 005 completion checker require Wilku's explicit 15-minute verdict to move into full content UAT before reporting complete_with_uat.
acceptance_criteria: Completion checker blocks valid ready-looking UAT when session_verdict is not 'przejdź do pełnego testu treści'; focused tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented Goal 005 completion gate on explicit ready session verdict and verified focused tests/CLI smoke.

title: Scope WordPress activation packet to selected content item
description: Make /api/content/wordpress/draft-activation-packet accept an optional work_item_id and make /content-workflow request the packet for the selected work item, avoiding mismatched activation state in the operator UI.
acceptance_criteria: API returns activation packet for ?work_item_id when provided, dashboard client encodes/passes selected work item ID, tests cover selected-item behavior, no write/publish path is enabled.
close_reason: Scoped activation packet to selected work item and verified API/dashboard behavior.

title: Show WordPress draft activation packet in dashboard
description: Render the read-only WordPress draft activation packet on /content-workflow so Wilku sees draft package ready, missing review/audit/handoff, dry-run blocked and live write disabled.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard fetches /api/content/wordpress/draft-activation-packet through typed schema, renders draft package/review/audit/handoff/dry-run/live-write state in Polish, focused dashboard/schema tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented typed dashboard activation packet panel and verified with focused test/typecheck.

title: Align WordPress mutation readiness with activation packet
description: Use the WordPress draft activation packet as the source of truth for ActionObject mutation readiness so the first write candidate does not report missing draft package when the content workflow snapshot already has one.
acceptance_criteria: Mutation readiness first write candidate targets the activation packet work item, reports wordpress_draft_package_ready satisfied when the packet has a draft package, removes missing_wordpress_draft_package_ready, keeps vendor writes at zero, and focused tests/live smoke pass.
close_reason: done

title: Expose WordPress draft activation packet
description: Add a read-only WILQ API packet for the first WordPress draft-only activation path. It must reuse the existing content workflow snapshot and wordpress-draft-execution dry-run contract, show whether draft package/handoff/dry-run are ready, list exact blockers/next steps, and keep live write/publish blocked.
acceptance_criteria: GET endpoint returns contract wordpress_draft_activation_packet_v1 for the current snapshot; reports draft_package_ready=true when snapshot has a package; reports missing human review/audit before handoff; never attempts external write; focused API tests pass.
close_reason: done

title: Block Goal 005 completion on follow-up-needed UAT
description: Harden scripts/goal_005_completion_check.py so a valid Goal 005 UAT result with needs_follow_up_before_full_content_uat remains evidence but does not produce complete_with_uat. Completion through UAT now requires ready_for_full_content_uat, otherwise owner defer is required.
close_reason: Completion checker now blocks follow-up-needed UAT results; focused pytest, ruff and CLI smokes passed.

title: Collect source-backed Ekologus service and claim source pack
description: Before expanding content knowledge cards, collect reviewed source material for real Ekologus services, buyer problems, claim boundaries, CTA preferences and evidence requirements. Current cards are seeded Goal 004 guardrails, not production-depth service knowledge.
design: No card expansion from memory. Source pack should identify each service/topic, source URL or reviewed owner note, allowed claims, claims needing human review, forbidden claims, CTA options and freshness date. Keep secrets and private client data out of committed docs.
acceptance_criteria: A source pack or explicit source blocker exists. It names BDO, waste obligations/reporting, environmental support/audit and any Zielony Lad/shop/product topics only if source-backed. It states what can safely be committed and what remains private/local proof.
close_reason: Source-backed Ekologus source pack recorded in docs/audits/005-2026-07-01-ekologus-source-pack.md; public ekologus.pl sources classified as commit-safe candidates, internal/private sources separated, and review-gated claim/CTA/evidence constraints documented for the next card-expansion slice.

title: Run first real Wilku content UAT or record explicit defer
description: Prepare and run one real Wilku content operations session after Goal 004: queue selection, enrichment, knowledge cards, Sales Brief v2, draft variant, quality review, revision, human review, WordPress draft-only dry-run and measurement plan. If owner defers, record residual risk explicitly.
design: UAT validates usefulness, not just schema shape. Capture whether Wilku understands why a topic matters, trusts source facts, sees where knowledge cards are missing, and can complete the session within 45 minutes without developer intervention.
acceptance_criteria: A UAT proof or owner-defer artifact exists with participant/date/scope, selected work item, blockers, time-to-complete, confusion points, off-brand/generic SEO findings, source-trace questions, and exact product follow-ups.

title: Audit Ekologus knowledge-card depth
description: Inspect current typed content knowledge cards and prove whether they cover real Ekologus services, buyer problems, triggers, CTA patterns, claim constraints and evidence requirements beyond Goal 004 seed cards. Identify placeholder/thin coverage and add focused blockers/tests where missing-card states must stop Sales Brief/draft work.
design: Typed cards first, no broad RAG/vector DB. Audit source lineage, confidence and freshness. Do not fix shallow knowledge with prompts or React copy. If cards are too thin, create precise follow-up implementation work instead of pretending UAT is ready.
acceptance_criteria: A coverage audit exists with card count, source lineage, missing service/claim/CTA/evidence gaps, stop-condition result, and exact next cards needed. Tests or planned tests prove missing required cards block Sales Brief/draft flow.
close_reason: Knowledge-card depth audit recorded in docs/audits/005-2026-07-01-knowledge-depth-audit.md; current cards classified as typed seeds/thin coverage, gaps and exact next cards named, follow-up tasks wilq-seo-ciz/wilq-seo-lt1/wilq-seo-t13 created, focused knowledge-card tests passed.

title: Goal 005 recovery and plan alignment
description: Align durable docs and recovery state around Goal 005 after Goal 004 completion. Update PLANS.md, docs/PROGRESS.md and docs/goals/005-goal.md so the active plan is restartable without chat history and clearly states UAT residual risk.
design: Docs-only activation slice. Do not implement product behavior. Use Beads for operational graph; markdown only describes objective, constraints, proof and recovery index.
acceptance_criteria: PLANS.md names Goal 005 as active, Goal 004 as completed baseline, docs/PROGRESS.md records the transition, docs/goals/005-goal.md defines objective/scope/verification/stop conditions, git diff --check passes, changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Goal 005 recovery docs and Beads graph aligned; docs-only verification passed with git diff --check.

title: Goal 005: Ekologus Knowledge Depth & UAT Closure
description: Validate that Goal 004's safe content operations loop is useful for Wilku with real Ekologus knowledge, not only mechanically complete. Scope: knowledge-card depth, read-only Service Profile/review path, first real Wilku UAT or explicit owner defer with residual risk, and Sales Brief v2 signal-quality audit. Do not add new writing flows, autopublish, broad RAG, social, workspace SaaS or outcome claims.
design: WILQ API remains the product brain. Goal 005 must deepen typed Ekologus knowledge and UAT proof without moving business logic to skills, React or prompts. Knowledge updates must be reviewed/gated; initial Service Profile work is read-only plus flagging/review, not ungated self-service writes.
acceptance_criteria: Goal 005 is complete only when knowledge-card coverage is audited against real Ekologus services/claims/triggers, missing-card blockers are proven, Wilku UAT is completed or explicitly owner-deferred with residual risk, Sales Brief v2 signal quality is assessed against live connector freshness/density, and docs/Beads/proof are aligned. Full verification runs before completion.

title: Goal 004 recovery and plan alignment

close_reason: Activated Goal 004 with docs/goals/004-goal.md, PLANS.md, docs/PROGRESS.md and Beads graph; git diff --check passes.

title: Goal 004: Content Operations Layer
description: Turn the verified Goal 003 Content Quality Workbench into a daily Ekologus content operating loop: opportunity enrichment, typed Ekologus knowledge cards, operations-grade Sales Brief, draft variants, bounded revision application, WordPress draft-only adapter boundary, measurement outcome interpretation, and a Codex content operator skill over WILQ API. This goal must keep WILQ API as the product brain, use OpenAI SDK Structured Outputs only after WILQ gates, forbid autopublish and broad RAG, and preserve no deprecated aliases when direct migration is feasible.
design: No MVP mindset: build the production-grade operating loop in small verified slices. React renders API-owned view models. Codex orchestrates and evaluates but is not the production writer. WordPress remains draft-only/review-gated. Typed knowledge cards/rules/validators/evals precede any retrieval layer.
acceptance_criteria: Goal 004 is complete when Wilku can run a real content work session from queue item selection through enrichment, typed knowledge cards, operations-grade Sales Brief, claim-gated draft variants, deterministic quality review, bounded revision application, human review, audit, WordPress draft-only handoff, measurement window and later outcome interpretation, with a WILQ content operator skill and full verification proof.
close_reason: Goal 004 completed: content operations loop, enrichment, typed knowledge cards, Sales Brief v2, draft variants, bounded revision, WordPress draft-only boundary, measurement outcome interpreter, content operator skill/UAT harness, UI/API hardening and full rtk scripts/verify.sh proof are complete.

description: Build the next WILQ content product layer after Goal 002: multi-item content queue, gated live Structured Outputs generation, deterministic quality review, revision plan, marketer workflow and draft-only WordPress boundary without SEO slop.

title: Goal 002: Content Production Engine bez slopu
description: Build the next WILQ product layer for Ekologus content production without SEO slop or technical slop. The goal is an API-first workflow from evidence through content inventory, preflight, preserve-first planning, sales brief, claim ledger, draft package, human review, WordPress draft handoff and measurement window.

Focused end-to-end proof and content suite pass. No WordPress write or publication is attempted.
