description: Harden ContentClaimLedger so forged or manually assembled entries cannot mark guarantees, legal/risk/environmental claims or measurement-dependent claims as generally allowed. This supports Goal 006 claim gating without changing draft generation flow.
acceptance_criteria: claim_ledger_blockers rejects guarantee_claim unless blocked; rejects legal/risk/environmental allowed statuses without reviewer_id; rejects performance/SEO/business outcome allowed_general before measurement; publish_ready_claims and claim_ledger_allows_draft respect these blockers; focused tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented Claim Ledger status/type consistency blockers with focused tests.

title: Tie WordPress handoff audit evidence to review and draft package
description: Goal 006/005 safety slice: WordPress draft handoff requires non-empty audit evidence, but any evidence ID currently satisfies the audit. Harden audit lineage so handoff is blocked unless audit evidence overlaps with the approved human review evidence and draft package evidence map.
acceptance_criteria: WordPress draft handoff returns audit_evidence_mismatch when audit evidence does not overlap human review evidence or draft package evidence. Existing valid handoff remains draft-only and passes. Focused tests prove both paths.
close_reason: WordPress handoff now blocks audit_evidence_mismatch when audit evidence does not overlap approved human review evidence or draft package evidence; focused tests pass.

title: Block structured preview claims outside generation contract
description: Goal 006 slice: structured draft preview validates evidence and claims_needing_review, but it does not block sections[].claims_used that are absent from contract.model_input.claims_allowed. Add a preview blocker before quality review so model/runtime output cannot introduce a new claim outside the WILQ generation contract.
acceptance_criteria: Structured draft preview returns a blocker when output sections use a claim not present in contract.model_input.claims_allowed. API/domain tests prove the blocker, while allowed claims still preview.
close_reason: Structured draft preview now blocks unknown_claim_reference when output claims are absent from contract.model_input.claims_allowed; domain and API tests pass.

title: Block draft claims outside Claim Ledger
description: Goal 006 slice: quality review currently blocks claim ledger blockers and forbidden claim leaks, but a structured draft can include a claims_used string that is not present in the Claim Ledger at all. Add an API-owned quality finding so drafts using unsupported/out-of-ledger claims are blocked before human review or WordPress handoff.
acceptance_criteria: Quality review returns a blocker when structured_output.sections[].claims_used contains a claim not present in ContentClaimLedger.entries. Tests prove the blocker, claim_safety blocked dimension, and no regression for allowed ledger claims.
close_reason: Quality review now blocks unsupported_claim_used for draft claims absent from Claim Ledger; adversarial API test proves blocked verdict and claim_safety dimension.

title: Re-run wilq-content-operator non-interactive eval after Codex usage reset
description: Follow-up from wilq-seo-3ii. The new wilq-content-operator eval case and harness guard are in place, static coverage passes, and the first Codex eval caught/fixed validated-without-action_id behavior. Re-run CODEX_SKILL_EVAL_IGNORE_USER_CONFIG=1 rtk scripts/codex_skill_eval.sh --skill wilq-content-operator --api-base http://127.0.0.1:8000 after the usage window resets. Previous blocker: Codex usage limit says try again at 10:23 PM.
acceptance_criteria: The non-interactive eval for wilq-content-operator passes, or the ledger records the concrete remaining grader/product failure with output path and follow-up.
close_reason: Re-run attempted twice; eval remains externally blocked by Codex usage limit. Recorded trace paths and classification in docs/evals/skill-eval-ledger.md.

title: Implement read-only Service Profile endpoint and dashboard
description: Follow-up from wilq-seo-94k. Implement the API-owned read-only Service Profile surface from docs/architecture/service-profile-review-surface.md: backend view model over existing knowledge cards/source facts, shared schema, dashboard API helper and Polish dashboard route/panels. V1 must not allow card edits, fact promotion or persistent review writes.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/content/service-profile returns typed read_only=true response with review_policy, production_depth_readiness, coverage_summary, service_sections, coverage_gaps and review_actions. Shared schema parses fixtures. Dashboard renders Polish Service Profile with no edit/promote controls. Tests prove seeded/review-required states, water-permit gap and blocked write policy.
close_reason: Implemented read-only Service Profile endpoint and dashboard. Added API-owned GET /api/content/service-profile, shared schema, dashboard API helper, /service-profile route, tests for read-only policy/review-required state/water-permit gap, and live smoke after stack restart.

title: Design private source proposal protocol for WILQ
description: Follow-up from private krn-ekologus-brain audit. Define a WILQ-local/redacted source proposal schema and review protocol for private Ekologus materials: metadata-only inventory, owner/audience/risk, schema-gated condensation, owner review, import proof, eval and lifecycle mapping to review_required source facts. Must not ingest raw client documents, full paths, filenames, emails, phone numbers, credentials or protected snippets into committed wilq-seo artifacts. Should feed read-only Service Profile review before any production-depth card promotion.
acceptance_criteria: A design doc or schema proposal exists for private source proposals. It states allowed fields, forbidden fields, privacy_class/review_status defaults, lifecycle promotion rules, eval requirements and how Service Profile can display review-required/private-source coverage without exposing raw protected content.
close_reason: Private source proposal protocol designed in docs/architecture/private-source-proposal-protocol.md. It defines allowed/forbidden fields, defaults for private_candidate/redacted review_required proposals, lifecycle promotion, Service Profile display constraints and eval requirements without importing raw private materials.

title: Add reviewed knowledge cards for water permit and waste storage content
description: Sales Brief v2 signal audit found current queue candidates 'operat wodnoprawny' and 'magazynowanie odpadów' have GSC+WordPress evidence, ready enrichment and measurement plan, but Sales Brief blocks with missing_required_knowledge_card. Add reviewed/source-backed service or claim-policy cards from public/reviewed Ekologus sources, or document a precise source blocker.
notes: Added source-backed review-required waste/packaging knowledge for magazynowanie odpadów; operat wodnoprawny remains blocked pending direct public/reviewed source. Live API proof: waste brief builds with draft_allowed=false, water permit blocks with missing_required_knowledge_card.
close_reason: Completed: waste storage now has source-backed review-required knowledge and live proof; water permit blocker documented pending direct source.

title: Tighten wilq-ads-doctor for fresh full-queue operator output
description: User-provided Ads skill output is safe but too report-like and was based on stale/default-compacted context. Update the skill contract/eval guidance so broad Ads questions use fresh vendor_read data or an explicit refresh blocker, request full_context when the user asks for the full review queue, and return prioritized operator decisions instead of field dumps.
close_reason: Tightened Ads skill contract/eval for freshness-first full queue and verified smoke/non-interactive eval

title: Add non-interactive eval case for wilq-content-operator
description: The OpenAI-aligned skill eval coverage audit reports wilq-content-operator as a skill directory without a dedicated eval case. Add a realistic Polish operator case that exercises content operations end-to-end: queue/enrichment/brief/quality review/human review/WordPress draft-only, with evidence IDs, source connectors, blocked claims, freshness handling and safe next step.
close_reason: Added realistic wilq-content-operator non-interactive eval case and tightened codex_skill_eval harness so validation_state=validated requires a non-empty action_id. Smoke passed and static coverage audit now covers 13/13 WILQ skills with zero warnings. First Codex eval found the validated-without-action_id issue; re-run is tracked in wilq-seo-kjf because Codex usage limit blocked the second run until 10:23 PM.

title: Harden WILQ skill eval layer with OpenAI-aligned gates
description: Add explicit OpenAI-aligned evaluation contract for WILQ skills and a deterministic coverage audit so BDOS-style operator commands are judged for evidence, blockers, usefulness and workflow-specific next steps, not just schema compliance.
close_reason: Implemented OpenAI-aligned skill eval contract, coverage audit, usefulness threshold and freshness handling gate

title: Record API recovery and GA4 traffic-quality proof
description: WILQ API became reachable again during Goal 005. Record the live GSC/GA4/WordPress refresh proof and wilq-ga4-analyst traffic-quality/measurement-blocker proof in recovery docs, without claiming ROAS, revenue or conversion conclusions.
acceptance_criteria: docs/PROGRESS.md, PLANS.md and a handoff artifact record API reachability, refresh run IDs, GA4 evidence IDs, WordPress refresh result, skill smoke proof and unsupported claims blocked.
close_reason: Completed: API recovery, live refresh IDs and wilq-ga4-analyst proof recorded in docs/PROGRESS.md, PLANS.md and docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-ga4-traffic-quality-proof.md

title: Goal 006 candidate: WILQ Claim Ledger and Generation Gate
description: Implement typed per-work-item Claim Ledger and Generation Gate in WILQ API, inspired by ekologus-ai evidence_pack/source_claim_markers/generation_gate. The gate must block unsupported draft claims, review-required knowledge misuse, legal/offer/product/penalty claims without human review, and publish/CMS-ready semantics before human review. This should extend the existing content workflow, not create a separate Wilku packet product layer.
acceptance_criteria: API exposes allowed, weak, forbidden and required claims for a content work item; structured draft runtime/quality review consumes the ledger; tests cover review-required source facts, seed-only cards, broad overmatch, product CTA without Merchant evidence, legal/current-law claim without review and draft claim outside the ledger.
close_reason: Claim Ledger and Generation Gate implementation is already complete: audit pass 13/13 and focused content gate tests pass 65/65. Remaining production-depth proof stays under Goal 005 Wilku/owner review and measurement windows.

title: Audit abandoned ekologus-ai reuse for WILQ content brain
description: Analyze https://github.com/rekurencja/ekologus-ai, especially src/ekologus_brain and approved materials, to identify reusable contracts/patterns for WILQ Goal 005 and broader BDOS-class content MOS. Output must separate what to port into typed WILQ API/source facts/gates from what to avoid copying, without ingesting raw private or sensitive content.
close_reason: Completed: roadmap and ekologus-ai reuse audit recorded in docs/roadmap/bdos-class-wilq-master-roadmap.md and docs/audits/005-2026-07-01-ekologus-ai-reuse-audit.md

title: Evaluate private Ekologus Brain source catalog relevance for Goal 005
description: User noted that a separate local project /home/krn/coding/krn/active/krn-ekologus-brain and internal Ekologus knowledge bases exist. This is only a potential future private source catalog, not something to connect automatically with WILQ SEO. Evaluate later whether any sanitized, reviewed outputs are worth using for Goal 005 knowledge review. Do not ingest client attachments, emails, phone numbers, credential paths or raw customer documents into wilq-seo, and do not build a bridge unless a later goal explicitly promotes it.
close_reason: Private krn-ekologus-brain source catalog evaluated without importing raw private materials. Audit recorded in docs/audits/005-2026-07-01-ekologus-brain-source-catalog-audit.md; reusable governed source-intake patterns mapped to WILQ Service Profile/source-fact work; follow-up wilq-seo-wtf created for private source proposal protocol.

title: Add knowledge-card production-depth guard tests
description: Add focused tests that distinguish seeded contract-proof cards from production-depth Ekologus knowledge. The current tests prove shape/basic blockers, but not whether source lineage/freshness/coverage are deep enough for daily content usefulness.
design: Guard source_lineage, freshness, service-specific coverage and missing-card blockers. Do not require arbitrary card counts unless tied to reviewed source pack. Prefer tests that prevent internal-only seeded cards from being described as production-depth.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if a production-depth readiness label can be returned from internal-only seeded cards, if broad environmental terms overmatch unrelated topics, or if missing service-specific CTA/claim/evidence cards do not block Sales Brief/draft readiness.
close_reason: Knowledge-card production-depth guards implemented: content knowledge responses expose production_depth_readiness, seeded cards return seeded_contract_proof/ready_for_daily_content=false, source-backed cards remain review-required, broad environmental terms no longer unlock service-card matching, and focused Python/shared/dashboard tests passed.

title: Expand typed Ekologus knowledge cards from reviewed sources
description: After the source pack exists, replace the broad seeded Goal 004 content knowledge coverage with reviewed, source-backed service, buyer-problem, trigger, CTA, claim-policy and evidence-requirement cards. Keep broad guardrail cards, but do not let them masquerade as production-depth service knowledge.
design: Implement in typed WILQ content knowledge modules with source_lineage, confidence and freshness. No broad RAG/vector DB. No prompt-only behavior. Cards must remain evidence companions, not evidence replacements.
acceptance_criteria: Knowledge card inventory includes reviewed service-specific cards and tests prove required service/CTA/claim/evidence cards block Sales Brief/draft when missing. Cards with only internal seeded lineage cannot unlock production-depth status.

title: Harden measurement outcome provenance for content decisions
description: Second-opinion follow-up after Goal 005 activation: verify and harden whether content measurement outcome interpretation is formally tied to metric_store facts, connector refresh/JobRun provenance, evidence IDs and the original content work item decision. Request typing for content POSTs is already fixed, so this task is about measurement provenance and false-success prevention, not Zod request schemas.
design: Keep outcome interpretation conservative. Do not create fake attribution science or SEO scores. Measurement hardening should make provenance explicit: which metric facts, connectors, refresh/job lineage, dates and work_item_id support an outcome. If live WILQ API is unreachable, work from local contracts/tests and record live-proof blocker.
acceptance_criteria: Audit or implementation proves one of: (a) outcome interpretation is tied to stored metric facts/job or connector refresh provenance with tests, or (b) a precise blocker states what metric_store/JobRun linkage is missing. Tests must prove no success/failure claim without metric evidence IDs, allowed metric names, source connectors, matching work_item_id/window and measurement readiness.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed measurement outcome provenance with metric_fact_ids, refresh_run_ids, matching work item/window/url, connector and allowed metric checks plus focused tests.

title: Define evidence-based draft variant selection guard
description: Goal 004 can produce draft variants, but Wilku still needs to understand which variant is safest/useful and why. Define a bounded selection/recommendation guard so variant choice is based on evidence coverage, service fit, buyer problem, CTA fit, duplicate risk and quality review findings, not the prettiest text.
design: Do not create a fake SEO score or new generation flow. API should expose variant comparison as blockers/dimensions/safe next step. React only renders the API-owned comparison.
acceptance_criteria: A design or implementation task exists for variant comparison with no magic score, no publish-ready claim, and tests planned/proven for generic-SEO variant blocking and evidence-bound recommended variant selection.
close_reason: Implemented API-owned draft variant selection guard with evidence dimensions and focused tests

title: Audit Sales Brief v2 signal quality
description: Assess whether enrichment inputs for Sales Brief v2 are dense, fresh and useful enough for real Ekologus content decisions. Separate connector freshness issues from model/card interpretation issues.
design: No fake SEO scores. Use WILQ evidence/source connector metadata, GSC/GA4/Ahrefs/WordPress signal density, service fit, buyer problem, CTA hypothesis and measurement baseline. Weak evidence must become a typed blocker or low-confidence state, not a polished generic brief.
acceptance_criteria: Audit identifies for current queue candidates whether signal quality is sufficient, thin, stale or blocked; names the weak connector/domain cause; and defines exact API/test changes needed before claiming daily content usefulness.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief v2 signal quality audit, fixed enrichment source-fact lineage and filed follow-ups for missing knowledge cards/blocked snapshot

title: Design read-only Ekologus Service Profile review surface
description: Define the first Service Profile path so Wilku can inspect current typed Ekologus knowledge, source lineage, freshness, claim rules and missing coverage without editing code. Initial product behavior is read-only plus flag/review request, not ungated self-service writes.
design: API-owned Service Profile view model. Dashboard renders Polish operator language. Any future write/update to knowledge cards requires human review/audit. No skill-only or React-only knowledge behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete API/dashboard design is ready for a read-only Service Profile with card status, source lineage, freshness, coverage gaps and flag-for-review semantics. It explicitly blocks direct knowledge writes and says what tests will prove it.
close_reason: Read-only Service Profile review surface designed in docs/architecture/service-profile-review-surface.md. The design defines API-owned GET /api/content/service-profile, Polish dashboard sections, coverage gaps, review request semantics, blocked write policy and tests proving no ungated knowledge edits or fact promotion.

title: Goal 004: collapse diagnostic UI and tighten API typing
description: Follow-up from the architecture audit: collapse diagnostic surfaces/content workflow UI into composable operator primitives, tighten dashboard API request typing/error handling, and quarantine or delete zombie copy tests/connectors without moving product logic into React. Prioritize DiagnosticSurface/SafetyGatePanel/DecisionCard primitives, content selected model extraction, typed Zod request schemas for content workflow POSTs, and clear experimental connector status.
close_reason: Completed UI/API hardening: typed dashboard POST requests, API error timeout/detail handling, diagnostic shell/SafetyGate/DecisionCard primitives, custom segment route decoupling, AdsDoctor extraction to focused components, operator safety guard consolidation, connector scope notes via API-owned risk_notes, frozen schemas clean, full rtk scripts/verify.sh passed.

title: Goal 004 wilq content operator skill and UAT harness
description: Create a WILQ content operator skill over WILQ API and a Wilku UAT harness for real content work sessions. The skill orchestrates queue, enrichment, draft variants, quality, revision, human review, draft-only handoff and measurement without becoming the writer.
design: Use skill-creator rules when implementing the skill. The skill consumes WILQ API only, refuses unsafe shortcuts, does not call OpenAI or WordPress directly, and must pass adversarial evals.
acceptance_criteria: Skill smoke/evals prove no direct content writing, no dev canonical, no publish, no skipped gates, no success claim before measurement and correct Polish operator output; UAT packet covers 3-5 real Ekologus work items.
close_reason: Implemented WILQ content operator skill and UAT harness over WILQ API; smoke proves queue/enrichment/knowledge/WP draft-only/measurement gates.

title: Goal 004 anti-slop execution guard
description: Keep Goal 004 from adding broad cleanup, monolith growth, prompt-only behavior, fake scores, stale aliases, broad RAG or React product logic while adding the operations layer.
design: Use changed-file budgets, targeted tests, contract inventory and frozen-growth checks. Add retrieval only after typed cards/rules/validators/evals are solid.
acceptance_criteria: Every Goal 004 slice records focused verification, no new feature growth in frozen monoliths, no deprecated alias retention when direct migration is feasible, and full scripts/verify.sh passes before completion.
close_reason: Goal 004 anti-slop guard satisfied: every slice recorded focused verification, frozen monolith growth stayed clean, no prompt-only/RAG/React product-brain behavior was added, and full rtk scripts/verify.sh passed before Goal 004 closure.

title: Goal 004 measurement outcome interpreter
description: Add conservative measurement outcome interpretation after measurement windows: not ready, insufficient data, noisy/inconclusive, directional improvement, likely underperformance, or measured success.
design: No success/failure before earliest verdict date. No causality theater. Outcome interpretation must feed future queue/knowledge decisions only with source evidence and confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove early windows block outcome claims, missing data returns insufficient/noisy states, and measured outcomes require source evidence, observation period and explicit limitations.
close_reason: Implemented conservative measurement outcome interpreter with typed measurement-outcome API, not_ready/insufficient/noisy/directional/underperformance/success states, early-claim blocking, evidence requirements and no causality theater. Proof: targeted measurement pytest subset, shared schema Vitest, Ruff, source mypy, Fallow audit, changed-file complexity audit and git diff --check.

title: Goal 004 draft variants through structured outputs
description: Add claim-gated draft variants for refresh, service-led, problem-led and FAQ/supporting content paths using WILQ API + OpenAI SDK Structured Outputs only after gates.
design: Variants are typed artifacts with evidence maps, blocked claims and publish_ready=false. No mass generation and no direct Codex writing.
acceptance_criteria: Fake SDK and API tests prove variants require gates, keep publish_ready=false, cite evidence/source connectors, reject forbidden claims and never write to WordPress.
close_reason: Implemented claim-gated draft variants via WILQ API and Structured Outputs contracts. Variants cover preserve-first refresh, problem-led, service-led and FAQ/supporting paths, keep publish_ready=false and wordpress_write_allowed=false, and pass fake SDK runtime proof plus focused tests.

title: Goal 004 WordPress draft-only adapter boundary
description: Harden the WordPress handoff path for optional real draft-only creation while keeping dry-run default and structurally blocking publish/destructive update.
design: No publish. No destructive update. Adapter must prove post_status=draft, publish_allowed=false, destructive_update_allowed=false, audit envelope present and external writes explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove dry-run default, blocked live without explicit config/review/audit, draft-only creation shape when enabled, no publish path and no destructive updates.
close_reason: Implemented explicit WordPress draft-only execution boundary with typed allowed operation, dry-run default, live adapter readiness, structural publish/destructive blocks, API blocked-live default and domain fake-adapter draft-only proof. Proof: targeted WordPress/content pytest subset, shared schema Vitest, dashboard API/route Vitest, dashboard typecheck, Ruff, source mypy, Fallow audit, changed-file complexity audit and git diff --check.

title: Goal 004 operations-grade Sales Brief from enrichment and knowledge

title: Add gated OpenAI SDK client for structured draft runtime
description: Goal 002 slice: wire the structured draft runtime to a real OpenAI SDK client only when live generation is explicitly enabled by runtime configuration. Inspect existing structured draft runtime, workflow API/router, env handling and tests first. Keep dry-run default, never write to WordPress, never publish, never expose raw payloads in marketer UI.

acceptance_criteria: The structured draft runtime endpoint keeps dry_run as default, blocks live mode when the enable flag is off, blocks live mode when the SDK/client/API key is unavailable, can call an injected/fake SDK client in tests, parses strict structured output, keeps publish_ready=false, and docs record that this does not publish or create WordPress drafts. Focused runtime/API tests, Ruff, mypy, complexity audit and diff-check pass.
close_reason: Added gated OpenAI SDK client boundary for structured draft runtime. Dry-run remains default; live requires WILQ_OPENAI_STRUCTURED_DRAFT_LIVE_ENABLED and SDK/API key, parses strict structured output through tests, keeps publish_ready=false and does not write to WordPress or publish.

Focused API/store/schema/dashboard checks, Ruff, mypy, typecheck/lint, Fallow and diff-check passed.

Snapshot GET may apply a stored valid review, but WordPress handoff must remain blocked until audit exists.

close_reason: Persisted snapshot Human Review path added: valid current-work-item review is stored in local SQLite and applied by later snapshot reads; wrong work-item review does not persist; WordPress handoff remains blocked without audit. Focused API/store/schema/dashboard checks, Ruff, mypy, typecheck/lint, Fallow and diff-check passed.

title: Goal 002 make content workflow snapshot stage-aware without synthetic human approval
description: Current diagnostics-derived /api/content/work-items/snapshot can render a full preview path, but it still builds an approved Human Review and prepared WordPress handoff from runtime data. Replace that with a stage-aware snapshot that shows current allowed/blocked stages without inventing human approval. Inspect Human Review API, WordPress handoff contract, ContentWorkflowSurface and workflow snapshot tests first.

Human review remains required before handoff_allowed; WordPress handoff result is blocked/null until review evidence exists; marketer-facing copy explains the blocker; focused API/dashboard/schema tests, typecheck/lint, Fallow audit and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Diagnostics-derived content workflow snapshot no longer fabricates approved human review, audit or WordPress handoff; API/dashboard/schema checks and live HTTP proof passed.

title: Goal 002 derive content workflow work items from inventory and diagnostics
description: Replace the temporary API-owned control snapshot with a real read-only content work item source derived from WILQ content inventory, diagnostics and preflight inputs. Inspect existing content workflow API, content diagnostics, inventory records, Content Planner dashboard and shared schemas first. Keep /content-workflow rendering API-owned view models; do not rebuild local React payload construction, do not generate drafts, do not call WordPress, and do not treat dev preview URLs as canonical.
design: WILQ API remains the product brain. Build a read-only work-item list/detail source that can select the first Ekologus content item from existing evidence/inventory/diagnostics, then reuse the existing workflow snapshot response shape. Avoid new feature behavior in frozen monoliths; create focused content/domain helpers or thin router code only.
acceptance_criteria: /content-workflow can load a work item selected from API-owned inventory/diagnostics rather than the hardcoded control snapshot; evidence IDs and source connectors persist; final canonical stays on ekologus.pl; WordPress remains draft-only; outcome claims remain blocked before measurement; focused API/dashboard/schema tests, typecheck/lint, Fallow audit and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed diagnostics-derived content workflow snapshot and removed the public control-snapshot fallback.

title: Goal 002 source content workflow from API-owned work item
description: Replace the first controlled /content-workflow dashboard payload with an API-owned ContentWorkItem source so React no longer constructs the BDO control work item locally. Inspect existing content workflow API, dashboard helpers and shared schemas first. Add the smallest endpoint/helper needed to fetch or select a content work item plus its workflow snapshot without adding behavior to frozen ContentDiagnosticSurface or main.py.
design: Keep WILQ API as the product brain. The dashboard route should render an API-owned work item/snapshot and stay a thin surface. Do not generate drafts, publish to WordPress, add deprecated aliases, use dev URL as canonical, or add local label remappers.
acceptance_criteria: /content-workflow renders from an API-owned content workflow source, not local hardcoded work item construction; tests prove evidence/source connectors persist, WordPress remains draft-only, outcome claims remain blocked, and no raw endpoint/schema names show in marketer-facing copy. Focused API/dashboard tests, typecheck/lint, Fallow audit and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed API-owned content workflow control snapshot and rewired /content-workflow to fetch it from WILQ API.

title: Goal 002 add marketer-facing content workflow route
description: Add the first dashboard surface for the Goal 002 content production workflow so Wilku can see one API-owned content work item path without React inventing product logic.
design: Create a new focused ContentWorkflowSurface route that consumes existing typed dashboard API helpers and renders marketer-readable step status, blockers and safety text. Do not modify the frozen ContentDiagnosticSurface, do not generate content, do not publish, and do not add local label remappers beyond minimal display strings for layout.
acceptance_criteria: A focused route test proves /content-workflow renders the ordered workflow steps from API-owned contracts, shows that WordPress stays draft-only, shows measurement success claims are still blocked, and does not display raw endpoint paths or schema names. Dashboard test/typecheck/lint, Fallow audit and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed marketer-facing /content-workflow route with ordered API-owned content workflow steps, draft-only WordPress handoff copy, measurement outcome blocker copy, focused route test, dashboard typecheck/lint, Fallow audit and diff-check proof.

title: Goal 002 expose content workflow contracts to dashboard client
description: Expose the Goal 002 content workflow API chain through shared Zod schemas and the dashboard API client so the dashboard can consume WILQ-owned contracts instead of local React product logic.
design: Add shared-schemas contracts for ContentWorkItem workflow request/response objects and thin dashboard api.ts helpers for the new /api/content/work-items/* endpoints. Do not build dashboard UI in this slice and do not add React label remappers.
acceptance_criteria: Shared schema tests parse preflight, sales brief, draft package, human review, WordPress draft handoff and measurement window response shapes. Dashboard API client tests prove helpers call the correct endpoint paths and parse responses. pnpm focused tests, typecheck/lint for touched packages as needed, fallow audit, git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed typed dashboard API boundary for Goal 002 content workflow: shared schemas parse preflight, sales brief, draft package, human review, WordPress draft handoff and measurement window responses; dashboard api.ts exposes thin POST helpers for all /api/content/work-items/* endpoints. Tests, typecheck, lint, Fallow audit and git diff --check passed; Fallow inherited index.ts duplicate findings remain excluded by gate.

title: Goal 002 add content workflow API chain smoke
description: Add one focused API chain smoke proving the Goal 002 content workflow endpoints can be called in order for one Ekologus work item without skipping gates: preflight, sales brief, draft package, human review, WordPress draft handoff and measurement window.
design: Use existing focused content workflow API tests and fixtures. Do not add a fake orchestrator endpoint, do not mutate product state in React, and do not claim live WordPress or live marketing outcomes. The test should exercise existing endpoints and assert every later step depends on typed artifacts from earlier steps.
acceptance_criteria: Focused test proves one content work item can move through API endpoints preflight -> sales brief -> draft package -> human review -> WordPress draft handoff -> measurement window. The test asserts evidence IDs persist, draft publish_ready=false, WordPress publish_allowed=false/destructive_update_allowed=false, measurement window planned with success_claim_allowed=false and early outcome blockers present. Ruff, pytest subset, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed focused content workflow API chain smoke: one fixture-backed Ekologus work item now goes through preflight, sales brief, draft package, human review, WordPress draft handoff and measurement window endpoints. The proof asserts evidence persistence, publish_ready=false, WordPress publish_allowed=false/destructive_update_allowed=false, measurement success_claim_allowed=false and early outcome blockers. Focused pytest, Ruff, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content measurement window API
description: Expose Content Measurement Window v1 through the focused content workflow API so a content work item can schedule the post-handoff observation window without allowing early success/failure claims.
design: Extend wilq/content/workflow/api.py and apps/api/wilq_api/routers/content_workflow.py. Reuse wilq.content.measurement.window build/apply/outcome helpers. Do not add measurement logic to frozen monoliths, do not claim outcomes before earliest_verdict_date, and keep React out of product decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/measurement-window returns a planned window for a valid item/handoff, applies measurement_window_status/id to the returned work item, blocks missing metrics, blocks missing source connectors, blocks dev preview canonical, keeps success_claim_allowed=false while planned, and exposes outcome blockers before earliest_verdict_date. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Measurement Window API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/measurement-window calls the typed Content Measurement Window v1 builder, returns a planned window or blockers, applies measurement_window_status/id only when created, keeps success_claim_allowed=false while planned, exposes early outcome blockers, and blocks missing metrics, missing connectors and dev canonical. Focused API/domain tests, Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content WordPress draft handoff API
description: Expose WordPress Draft Handoff v1 through WILQ API after Draft Package and approved Human Review. This should prepare a typed draft-only handoff contract without writing to WordPress or publishing.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, Draft Package, Human Review and WordPress draft audit envelope, call build_content_wordpress_draft_handoff, and return typed handoff/blockers. Do not call WordPress connector, do not create a post, do not publish and do not allow destructive updates.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/wordpress-draft-handoff returns a prepared draft-only handoff for valid item/review/audit, blocks missing audit, blocks non-approved review, blocks dev preview canonical, keeps publish_allowed=false and destructive_update_allowed=false, and preserves evidence IDs. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed WordPress Draft Handoff API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/wordpress-draft-handoff calls the typed prepare-only builder, returns prepared draft-only handoff or blockers, and never calls WordPress or publishes. Focused API tests prove valid prepared handoff, missing audit blocker, non-approved review blocker, dev canonical blocker, publish_allowed=false, destructive_update_allowed=false and evidence preservation; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content human review API
description: Expose Human Review v1 through WILQ API so a Draft Package can be approved or blocked by a typed human decision before any WordPress draft handoff.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, ContentHumanReview, optional Draft Package and optional Claim Ledger, call content_human_review_blockers and apply_content_human_review_to_work_item, and return typed blockers, reviewed item state and whether WordPress handoff is allowed. Do not create WordPress handoff or write to WordPress.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/human-review updates the work item for approved draft review, blocks missing reviewer/checklist/evidence, blocks needs_changes from WordPress handoff, requires blocked claims to be handled, and returns no WordPress handoff. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Human Review API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/human-review calls typed Human Review v1 blockers, updates ContentWorkItem only after blocker-free review, reports wordpress_handoff_allowed without creating handoffs. Focused API tests prove approved review state update, missing reviewer/checklist/evidence blockers, needs_changes blocker and blocked-claim handling; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content draft package API
description: Expose Draft Package v1 through WILQ API after ContentWorkItem preflight and Sales Brief. This moves content workflow from API-visible brief to API-visible outline-first draft package without WordPress handoff or publication.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, inventory records/duplicate risk, Claim Ledger, Sales Brief seed facts and optional supplied Sales Brief. It should compute inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, build or use the Sales Brief, then call build_content_draft_package. Return typed preflight, sales brief result and draft package result. Do not generate full prose with an LLM, do not call Codex, do not create WordPress drafts, and keep publish_ready=false.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/draft-package returns an outline-first not-publish-ready Draft Package for a valid draft_allowed item, blocks when preflight is not draft_allowed, blocks when Claim Ledger has unresolved/measurement-blocked claims, preserves section-to-evidence mapping, and returns no WordPress handoff. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Draft Package API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/draft-package computes inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, builds or accepts Sales Brief v1, then calls the typed Draft Package v1 builder. Focused API tests prove outline-first publish_ready=false package creation, preflight-not-draft-allowed blocking, Claim Ledger blocking, no WordPress handoff and section-to-evidence mapping; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content sales brief API
description: Expose the existing Sales Brief v1 domain contract through WILQ API after ContentWorkItem preflight. This moves the content workflow from API-visible preflight to API-visible brief generation without adding prompt-only drafting or React-side product logic.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, inventory records/duplicate risk, Claim Ledger and Sales Brief seed facts, compute inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, then call build_content_sales_brief. It must return typed preflight and Sales Brief build result, preserving evidence IDs/source connectors and blocking brief creation when preflight or final canonical/measurement/source facts are invalid.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/sales-brief returns a typed brief for a valid preserve-first item with evidence-backed source facts, blocks dev preview canonical or missing source facts, preserves forbidden claims from claim ledger, and does not create draft/WordPress artifacts. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/sales-brief computes Content Inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, then calls the typed Sales Brief v1 builder; focused API tests prove valid preserve-first brief generation, missing source-fact blockers, forbidden claim visibility, and no draft/WordPress artifacts; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content work item preflight API
description: Expose the existing ContentWorkItem, Content Inventory v1 and ContentPreflight v2 domain contracts through a WILQ API endpoint without adding product logic to React, skills or frozen monolith files. This is the first API bridge from domain-only content workflow contracts to operator/runtime usage.
design: Create a focused router/module for content workflow API contracts. The endpoint should accept a typed ContentWorkItem plus inventory records/duplicate risk, call wilq.content.inventory.records.resolve_content_inventory and wilq.content.preflight.workflow.build_content_preflight_verdict, and return typed data with evidence/source connectors preserved. Do not add draft generation, WordPress writes, prompt logic, deprecated aliases or dev URL canonical exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: A focused API contract test proves POST /api/content/work-items/preflight returns blocked for dev/preview canonical, plan_allowed or brief_allowed for a valid preserve-first item, evidence IDs and source connectors are preserved, and no existing /api/content/preflight response shape changes. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed first content workflow API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/preflight exposes ContentWorkItem, Content Inventory v1 and ContentPreflight v2 through a focused router/domain API model; focused API tests prove dev preview canonical is blocked, valid preserve-first item preserves evidence/source connectors and existing GET /api/content/preflight shape stays unchanged; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 Content Measurement Window v1
description: Add a post-publication measurement window model for content work items so WILQ cannot claim success or failure before evidence exists.
design: Measurement window records baseline period, observation period, earliest verdict date, allowed metrics, source connectors and current status. No success/failure language before the window is ready for review.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove content outcome claims are blocked before the measurement window closes and that every WordPress draft handoff schedules or requires a measurement window.
close_reason: Completed Content Measurement Window v1 baseline: added measurement window contract with baseline period, observation period, earliest verdict date, allowed metrics, source connectors, evidence IDs and optional WordPress handoff link. Outcome claims stay blocked before the window reaches ready_for_review/closed after earliest_verdict_date; handoff requires or schedules a measurement window. Checks passed: tests/content/test_measurement_window.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new measurement module, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and staged git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 WordPress Draft Handoff v1
description: Implement a gated WordPress draft handoff for approved content packages only, with audit and no publication.
design: The handoff creates or prepares a WordPress draft only after approved human review. No automatic publish, no destructive update of existing canonical content and no dev URL canonical target.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove no WordPress draft handoff without human review, audit envelope, final canonical URL and safe draft status. Publish/apply remains blocked.
close_reason: Completed WordPress Draft Handoff v1 baseline: added prepare-only draft handoff contract requiring public final canonical URL, matching Draft Package, approved Human Review and audit envelope with evidence IDs. Handoff blocks dev preview canonical targets, non-approved review, missing audit, mismatched draft packages and publish-ready draft packages; it emits post_status=draft, publish_allowed=false and destructive_update_allowed=false, and only marks workflow draft_created when a real WordPress post ID is provided. Checks passed: tests/content/test_wordpress_handoff.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on new handoff/workflow modules, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Human Review v1
description: Add explicit human review records for content brief, claim ledger, draft package and WordPress draft handoff decisions.
design: Robot cannot mark content publish-ready. Review events must record reviewer, decision, notes, checked items, evidence IDs and blocked claims handled.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove WordPress draft handoff is blocked without approved human review, and review results are available to the content workflow state.
close_reason: Completed Human Review v1 baseline: added typed content human review records for sales brief, claim ledger, draft package and WordPress handoff stages. Review requires reviewer, decision, checked items, evidence IDs and handled blocked claims; only approved review can update ContentWorkItem to human_review_status=approved and unblock the WordPress handoff gate. Checks passed: tests/content/test_human_review.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on new review/workflow modules, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Draft Package v1
description: Create a typed draft package workflow that can produce outline-first content only after preflight, sales brief and claim ledger gates pass.
design: Draft packages are not publish-ready. They must include brief_id, claim_ledger_id, section_to_evidence_map, claims_used, claims_removed_or_blocked, human_review_questions and publish_ready=false.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove drafts are blocked without preflight, sales brief and claim ledger, and generated draft packages always include evidence mapping and human review questions.
close_reason: Completed Draft Package v1 baseline: outline-first draft packages now build only after draft_allowed preflight, matching Sales Brief, matching Claim Ledger and source-fact evidence mapping. Packages include brief_id, claim_ledger_id, section-to-evidence map, publish-ready claims, blocked claims removed, human review questions and publish_ready=false. Checks passed: tests/content/test_draft_package.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Claim Ledger v1
description: Add a structured claim ledger for content promises, SEO/performance claims, compliance/environmental-adjacent claims and business outcome claims.
design: Claims are data, not prompt instructions. Statuses include allowed_with_evidence, allowed_general, needs_human_review, blocked and blocked_until_measurement. Unsupported promises of rankings, leads, revenue, full legal compliance or guaranteed outcomes are blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove blocked claims cannot appear as publish-ready language and draft generation remains blocked when required claim review is missing.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Claim Ledger v1 in wilq/content/claims/ledger.py. Claims are structured entries with type, status, evidence IDs, reason and optional reviewer. Deterministic rules block guarantee claims, block SEO/performance/business outcome claims until measurement is ready, require human review for legal/risk/environmental claims and block allowed_with_evidence entries that have no evidence. Focused tests prove blocked claims are excluded from publish-ready claims and draft remains blocked when required claim review or evidence is missing. Checks passed: tests/content/test_claim_ledger.py, tests/content (67 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Claim Ledger v1 baseline: claims are structured data with allowed, review-required, blocked and blocked-until-measurement statuses; blocked or unreviewed risky claims cannot be publish-ready and draft remains blocked when claim review/evidence is missing.

title: Goal 002 Sales Brief v1
description: Create an API-owned sales/content brief contract for Ekologus content work items before any draft is generated.
design: The brief is not a prompt. It must include buyer problem, buyer trigger, target reader, search intent, service fit, source facts, evidence IDs, final canonical target, existing content plan, H1/H2/FAQ/CTA direction, internal links, forbidden claims, missing evidence and measurement plan.
acceptance_criteria: A sales brief endpoint/service can build a structured brief only from a valid work item/preflight. Tests prove no brief is produced without required evidence and final URL semantics.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Sales Brief v1 in wilq/content/briefs/sales.py. The brief is a typed contract built only from ContentWorkItem, ContentPreflight v2, Content Inventory v1, Claim Ledger v1 and an explicit seed with buyer problem, buyer trigger, target reader, search intent, service fit, source facts, H1/H2/FAQ/CTA direction, internal links, missing evidence and measurement plan. Focused tests prove no brief is produced without required evidence/source facts, valid final canonical semantics, preflight allowance or measurement plan, and that dev preview URLs are rejected as final canonical. Checks passed: tests/content/test_sales_brief.py, tests/content (74 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief v1 baseline: typed brief contract now builds only from valid work item/preflight/inventory/claim ledger inputs, with required evidence, final canonical URL, source facts, forbidden claims and measurement plan. No draft generation or React-side decision logic was added.

title: Goal 002 ContentPreflight v2
description: Turn the current 'Czy można pisać?' surface into a first-class API workflow verdict with blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states.
design: Preflight cannot draft or write. Draft is allowed only after sales brief and claim ledger. Handoff is allowed only after human review. React must render API-owned labels and disabled reasons.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests cover no evidence, missing connector, missing final canonical, dev URL as canonical, existing content preserve-first, duplicate risk, missing brief and missing human review.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added ContentPreflight v2 workflow verdict in wilq/content/preflight/workflow.py. The verdict exposes blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states over ContentWorkItem plus Content Inventory v1. Preflight still does not draft or write: it only exposes create_allowed, sales_brief_allowed, draft_allowed and wordpress_draft_allowed booleans with blockers. Focused tests cover no evidence, missing connector, missing final canonical, dev preview as canonical, existing content preserve-first, duplicate risk, missing brief, missing human review and full handoff allowed. Checks passed: tests/content/test_preflight_workflow.py, tests/content (61 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed ContentPreflight v2 domain verdict: blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states now compose ContentWorkItem and Content Inventory v1 without drafting, writing or React-side decision logic.

title: Goal 002 Content Inventory v1
description: Build a first content inventory layer that can answer what content exists on Ekologus public sources, what final canonical URL is known, and whether a topic should preserve, refresh, merge, create after review or block.
design: Inventory is a domain service, not React logic and not skill logic. Dev preview URLs are not historical SEO evidence and must not become final canonical URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Content inventory returns structured records with URL/canonical/status/source/evidence fields and focused tests cover existing URL, missing final canonical, dev preview as non-canonical and duplicate-risk inputs.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Content Inventory v1 records/resolution in wilq/content/inventory/records.py. Inventory now returns structured records and a resolution with URL, final canonical, preview, source connectors, evidence IDs, blockers and preserve/create/block mode. Focused tests cover existing public URL preserve-first, missing final canonical blocker, dev preview URL as invalid canonical, unresolved duplicate risk blocking create, clear duplicate check allowing create_after_review and canonical deduplication. Checks passed: tests/content/test_inventory_records.py, tests/content (52 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Content Inventory v1 baseline: structured inventory records and resolution now support preserve-first decisions, missing canonical blockers, dev-preview canonical rejection, duplicate-risk blockers and create_after_review only after a clear duplicate check.

title: Goal 002 extract API routers without behavior change
description: Split FastAPI router responsibilities out of apps/api/wilq_api/main.py while preserving endpoint paths and JSON response shapes.
design: This is behavior-preserving extraction only. main.py should move toward a thin app/entrypoint; no new content product behavior should be added in the extraction slice.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API contract tests and live smoke prove existing endpoints still respond with the same shape. main.py loses routing responsibility without changing marketer behavior.
notes:
Slice proof 2026-06-30: read-only connector endpoints moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into apps/api/wilq_api/routers/connectors.py without changing endpoint paths or response shapes. Connector refresh POST remains in main.py until cache invalidation is extracted safely. Focused checks passed: tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_connector_registry_contains_required_connectors, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_localo_status_requires_api_token_and_organization_id, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_connector_status_does_not_expose_secret_values, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_connector_refresh_run_persists_redacted_evidence, tests/test_metric_store_and_cli.py::test_metrics_api_exposes_metric_store_status_and_facts, tests/test_connector_status_labels.py, route-shape smoke for /api/connectors and connector status/refresh-run reads, Ruff on touched API files, mypy on the new router, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check. This is partial progress; keep issue open for more router extraction.
Slice proof 2026-06-30: jobs and job-run endpoints moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into apps/api/wilq_api/routers/jobs.py without changing endpoint paths or response shapes. Scheduler domain behavior stays in wilq.jobs; main.py still uses scheduler_status for system-status aggregation only. Focused checks passed: tests/test_jobs_scheduler.py, route-shape smoke for /api/jobs, /api/jobs/status, /api/jobs/{job_id}, /api/job-runs and 404 paths, Ruff on touched API files, mypy on routers/connectors.py and routers/jobs.py, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check. This is partial progress; keep issue open for more router extraction.
Slice proof 2026-06-30: evidence and metric read endpoints moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into apps/api/wilq_api/routers/evidence.py and apps/api/wilq_api/routers/metrics.py without changing endpoint paths or response shapes. Context-pack internals still read evidence/metric stores directly where they build compact operator context. Focused checks passed: tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_evidence_registry_exposes_connector_status_without_secret_values, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_actions_reference_registered_evidence_ids, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_connector_refresh_run_persists_redacted_evidence, tests/test_metric_store_and_cli.py::test_metrics_api_exposes_metric_store_status_and_facts, tests/test_metric_store_and_cli.py::test_metric_fact_evidence_ids_are_resolvable_without_refresh_run_state, route-shape smoke for /api/evidence and /api/metrics reads, Ruff on touched API files, mypy on evidence.py and metrics.py routers, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check. This is partial progress; keep issue open for more router extraction.
Slice proof 2026-06-30: knowledge and expert-rule endpoints moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into apps/api/wilq_api/routers/knowledge.py and apps/api/wilq_api/routers/expert.py without changing endpoint paths or response shapes. Context-pack compaction helpers remain in main.py for a later scoped extraction. Focused checks passed: tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_expert_rules_are_loaded_from_structured_files, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_expert_capabilities_are_available_through_api, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_knowledge_playbooks_are_machine_readable_and_evidence_gated, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_knowledge_compiler_produces_lineage_preserving_card_types, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_knowledge_operating_map_binds_sources_to_decisions, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_codex_context_pack_includes_expert_rule_summaries, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_codex_context_pack_includes_compiled_knowledge_cards, route-shape smoke for /api/knowledge and /api/expert reads, Ruff on touched API files, mypy on knowledge.py and expert.py routers, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check. This is partial progress; keep issue open for more router extraction.
Slice proof 2026-06-30: workflow and workflow-run endpoints moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into apps/api/wilq_api/routers/workflows.py without changing endpoint paths or response shapes. Codex run endpoints remain in main.py for a later context-runtime router slice. Focused checks passed: tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_workflows_are_decision_backed_operator_contracts, tests/test_api_contracts.py::test_workflow_run_persists_to_local_state_with_redaction, route-shape smoke for /api/workflows and /api/workflow-runs reads/writes/404 paths, Ruff on touched API files, mypy on workflows.py, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check via rtk proxy after fixing a trailing blank line. This is partial progress; keep issue open for more router extraction.

title: Goal 002 ContentWorkItem workflow state
description: Add a typed ContentWorkItem workflow model that tracks one content item from evidence through inventory, preflight, planning, brief, claims, draft, human review, WordPress handoff and measurement.
design: No deprecated target_site fields. The model must use source_public_url, final_canonical_url, intended_final_url and preview_url only where those concepts are real.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove the state machine blocks skipping preflight, sales brief, claim ledger, human review and audit gates.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added typed ContentWorkItem workflow state in wilq/content/workflow/models.py with state blockers for evidence, source connectors, inventory, public final canonical URL, duplicate gate, preflight, preserve-first plan, sales brief, claim ledger, draft package, human review, audit and measurement window. The workflow explicitly allows preparing useful content work before post-publication metrics, but blocks draft/handoff without a measurement plan and blocks outcome claims until the measurement window is ready. Focused checks passed: tests/content/test_workflow_state.py, tests/content (46 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed ContentWorkItem workflow state baseline: typed model and transition blockers now prove preflight, sales brief, claim ledger, human review, audit and measurement gates cannot be skipped. Draft/handoff require a measurement plan, while outcome claims remain blocked until the measurement window is ready.

title: Remove concurrent cold-build contention from the Knowledge surface
description:
## Problem
/knowledge uruchamia operating-map, cards i playbooks równolegle. Na zimnym managed runtime współbieżny odczyt powoduje 51.6 s dla operating-map i E2E timeout, mimo że każdy endpoint po rozgrzaniu odpowiada poniżej sekundy.

## Current Evidence
2026-07-10: focused knowledge-layout Playwright timeout 30.2 s czekając na /api/knowledge/operating-map. Po restarcie pojedynczy cold operating-map 16.52 s; przy równoległych cold cards/playbooks: cards 1.90 s, playbooks 1.69 s, operating-map 51.57 s. Payloady nie były drukowane.

## Out of Scope
Zmiana knowledge semantics/approval, nowy endpoint, publikacja wiedzy i refaktor całego GenericSurface.

## Dependencies and Required Tests
No active task covers cold contention. Reuse existing knowledge endpoints/builders. Required: request/build count, concurrent cold timing, deferred UI test, Playwright knowledge layout, knowledge lifecycle regression.

## Done
Pierwsza knowledge decision renderuje <=5 s; cards/playbooks nie konkurują z primary operating map i ładują się dopiero przy potrzebie albo ze współdzielonego cache/snapshotu.
design: Pogłębić wspólny knowledge snapshot/compiler cache seam albo sekwencjonować subordinate queries po primary map. GenericSurface najpierw renderuje map-owned decyzję, a cards/playbooks mają lokalne loading/disclosure. Nie dodawać duplikującego endpointu.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Jedno cold primary knowledge build <=5 s. 2. Concurrent subordinate reads nie wydłużają operating-map >5 s. 3. First decision renderuje przed katalogiem. 4. Cards/playbooks zachowują lineage/status po lokalnym load. 5. API/Vitest/Playwright i lifecycle tests przechodzą.
close_reason: Acceptance met: primary map build is under 5s at core, non-blocking prewarm removes API/process cold overhead without delaying startup health, subordinate catalogs are deferred, lineage remains covered, and API/Vitest/Playwright/lifecycle proof passes.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Slice 2026-07-11 started: GenericSurface now loads only operating-map initially; knowledge cards/playbooks queries are disabled until their disclosure buttons are opened, removing concurrent subordinate builds from the first decision. Existing operating-map endpoint now has 15s read-through cache; cache unit test passes. Cold HTTP after managed restart remains 18.940732s, warm 0.053012s, so no prewarm is kept in lifespan because the 18s build would block startup health; the route still renders the marketer-facing knowledge decision/blockers within the first 3s while map data is loading. Proof: .local-lab/proof/c9h9-12-knowledge-progressive-3s.png. Focused GenericSurface 4/4, dashboard lint/typecheck and Ruff/mypy pass. Remaining: reduce cold operating-map build below 5s or introduce a non-blocking safe prewarm without startup regression; do not claim map is fresh before it loads.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Follow-up measurement: standalone list_workflows() triggers build_daily_runtime and measured 8.519s in a fresh process; the subsequent build_daily_command_center() was 0.0s only because daily runtime caches were already warm. No safe semantic reduction was committed from this probe; map cold HTTP remains 18.940732s. Next implementation must target the actual operating-map build/cache or a non-blocking warm-up, not duplicate command-center endpoints.

comments.author: korneliuszburian

title: Load Custom Segments from a narrow Ads diagnostics view
description:
## Problem
/ads-doctor/custom-segments calls the full Ads diagnostics contract and does not render its heading within the 30 s Playwright budget, even after a clean managed-stack restart. Experimental route blocks its own E2E and contributes to dashboard queue latency.

## Current Evidence
2026-07-10 focused Playwright custom-segments test failed after 30.8 s waiting for Segmenty z haseł. CustomSegmentsDiagnosticSurface.tsx uses getAdsDiagnostics(), while AdsDoctorSurface already uses getAdsDiagnosticsSummary().

## Out of Scope
Campaign writes, audience creation/apply, invented audience sizes, refactor entire ads_diagnostics.py and priority work on /content-workflow.

## Dependencies and Required Tests
No active Bead covers this route latency. Reuse/extend existing /api/ads/diagnostics view boundary. Required: focused API view contract, Vitest, cold Playwright, no unsupported audience/ROAS claims.

## Done
Custom segments heading/decision renders <=5 s or exposes typed source blocker, while detailed candidate/forecast data remains evidence-backed and review-only.
design: Rozszerzyć istniejący Ads diagnostics view seam o minimalny custom-segments projection albo udowodnić, że summary może zachować wymagane typed fields. Dashboard nie może ładować pełnych kampanii/search terms/recommendations dla samej decyzji segmentowej. Bez nowego endpointu.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Route nie używa bezwarunkowo pełnego getAdsDiagnostics. 2. Narrow view zawiera status, source terms/candidates, missing contracts, forecast blocker, evidence IDs/connectors. 3. Cold first decision <=5 s. 4. Focused API/Vitest/Playwright przechodzą. 5. Audience size, ROAS i write pozostają zablokowane bez kontraktu.
close_reason: Closed 2026-07-11: CustomSegmentsDiagnosticSurface now consumes existing /api/ads/diagnostics?view=summary, which preserves typed custom-segment candidates, forecast, keyword-planner status, evidence and blocked claims without full Ads payload. Focused Playwright dashboard-api.spec.ts custom segments route passes 1/1 in 4.4s; dashboard lint/typecheck, Ads contracts and summary cache proof pass. No new endpoint, audience-size/ROAS claims or write path added.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Handoff: legacy full Ads diagnostics remains used only by /ads-doctor full detail; summary seam is the canonical narrow route input. Any future enrichment must extend summary view or a typed existing view, not revert to getAdsDiagnostics().

title: Stabilize Service Profile route test under the full dashboard suite
description:
## Problem
Pełny dashboard Vitest run nie jest deterministyczny: ServiceProfileSurface.test.tsx przechodzi izolowanie, ale w 24-file parallel run pierwszy findByText kończy się przed załadowaniem trasy. To blokuje canonical scripts/verify.sh i może ukryć realne regresje za losowym retry.

## Current Evidence
2026-07-10: isolated file 1/1 passed; dwa pełne runy zakończyły się odpowiednio globalnym 5000 ms timeoutem oraz TestingLibraryElementError po domyślnym około 1000 ms async query. Pozostałe 136 dashboard tests passed. Zmieniany WordPress/content zakres nie importuje ServiceProfileSurface.

## Out of Scope
Zmiana Service Profile UI/API, osłabianie asercji, globalne zwiększenie timeoutów, serializacja całego Vitest i refaktor routera.

## Dependencies and Required Tests
Discovered from c9h9.3 final verification. Focused ServiceProfile test, full 24-file dashboard Vitest, lint/typecheck and final remaining verify stages must pass.

## Done
Test czeka jawnie i lokalnie na async route render w realistycznym budżecie, nadal wykonuje wszystkie istniejące product assertions, przechodzi izolowanie oraz w pełnym równoległym dashboard runie.
design: Seam: tylko async readiness pierwszego renderu w ServiceProfileSurface.test.tsx. Użyć lokalnego Testing Library timeout/test budget bez zmiany produkcji, bez retry i bez usuwania asercji; zachować pełne zachowanie testu.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Pierwsza asercja route readiness ma jawny timeout adekwatny do pełnego parallel runu. 2. Żadna istniejąca asercja produktu nie jest usunięta ani poluzowana. 3. Izolowany test przechodzi. 4. Pełny dashboard Vitest ma 137/137. 5. Lint, typecheck i git diff --check przechodzą.
notes: 2026-07-10 completed: ServiceProfileSurface kept every product assertion and received only a local 10s async route-readiness wait plus 15s test budget. Isolated file passes 1/1; full parallel dashboard run passes 24 files and 137/137 tests; ESLint/typecheck pass. No production code or global timeout changed.
close_reason: Local async test budget stabilizes Service Profile without weakening assertions; isolated and full 137/137 dashboard tests pass.

acceptance_criteria: Daily-check safe next actions can be logged as recommendation records and later connected to measurement/learning without performing vendor mutation.

title: Goal 002 extract Codex context-pack runtime internals
description: Move heavy Codex context-pack construction helpers out of apps/api/wilq_api/main.py into a focused runtime/service module without changing /api/codex/context or /api/codex/context-pack response shapes.
design: This is behavior-preserving extraction after router extraction. Keep routers thin. Do not add content product behavior, prompt logic or dashboard labels. The service may depend on domain builders and stores, but no domain module may import apps.api.
acceptance_criteria: Focused Codex context-pack API tests and route smoke pass before and after extraction. main.py no longer owns context-pack helper internals beyond app wiring. Context-pack output remains API-owned, evidence-gated and redacted.
notes: Partial slice 2026-06-30: skill-scoped context-pack orchestration, skill diagnostics dispatch and skill opportunity filtering moved from apps/api/wilq_api/main.py to apps/api/wilq_api/context_skill.py. main.py now delegates non-daily skill context packs to that runtime module and no longer keeps per-skill routing/evidence/redaction flow locally. Focused checks passed: Ruff on main.py/context_skill.py, mypy on the same files, focused pytest for content, GSC, Ads, custom-segment, campaign-builder, Demand Gen, social, knowledge and expert skill context packs, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check. Issue remains open until full-context assembly is either extracted or explicitly deemed app wiring.
close_reason: Completed behavior-preserving context-pack runtime extraction: main.py delegates daily, skill-scoped and full-context context-pack construction to focused runtime modules; full-context assembly moved to apps/api/wilq_api/context_full.py; focused full-context tests, Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.
